title: Farne Island
description: A much cleaner, crisper beer and a pleasure to drink. Tasted like a good old fashioned IPA-style beer, although nothing spectacular.

title: Alnwick IPA
description: Sorry for the lack off-brand photo, I'm visiting Mum and Dad Ale House after their holiday (and drinking their beer). Not very impressed with this one, had a wholesome smell but an odd aftertaste (and taste in general). Not to keen on this one

title: Northern Mischief
description: A friend came round to play board games and bought a couple of @woohabrewing beers with him. This bottle conditioned lager was a crisp little number. Very lively (hence the big head) but light and refreshing. As with any lager, there was no notably "big" flavour.

title: American Pale Ale
description: The final @toastale I got from my mum and would say it was my second favourite. Crisp, light and easy drinking. Nice way to finish off an afternoon of drinking in the pub and accompanied dinner wonderfully. Wouldn't go out of my way to hunt one of these down, but would pick one up if I was passing

title: Stur
description: Another DIPA from @thebeakbrewery and another absolute banger. A bit more of a hoppy/harsh DIPA, not as smooth and hazy as the other ones I've had from them. The 8.5% snacks you in the face  with this one and leaves no prisoners. A perfect one-can-of-an-evening, kind of drink. Picked up at @palate.bottleshop - looks like I'll have to go back there soon too, to pick up more Beak beer!

title: Into the Haze
description: Another corker from @deyabrewery, fruity, refreshing and very easy drinking. Reminded me of cloudy apple juice in colour and texture but a light beer in taste. Could drink a few of these and fully appreciate the 500ml. Picked this IPA up from @palate.bottleshop a couple of weeks ago. Best served cold

title: Big Baller
description: Popped into the @craftbeercabin at the weekend to grab a park beer and this caught my eye as a take-home-and-try kind of beer. It was only when I got outside did I realise this TIPA from @gipsyhillbrew is 11.3%! Was a bit disappointed in how it poured - it was very sediment-y and this photo was taken after a while of letting it settle and pouring into a couple of glasses to try and reduce. This beer, for me, was very much a "try it" beer. The flavours were there (along with a very strong punch) but I won't be buying one again - no doubt marred by the pour

title: Jungle Trip
description: I didn't realise I was such a fan of New England IPAs until @marclittlemore asked for some recommendations and all the NEIPAs I had recently drunk scored highly. I grabbed this one when I saw it on the Tesco shelves and was not disappointed. It was smooth easy drinking with plenty of flavour and definitely one I will buy again. @londonbeerfactory

title: IPA
description: A simple no frills IPA @unbarredbrewery. Not overly fruity (which is a lovely change), nice and smooth with still the classic IPA tang. Great to have a run-of-the-mill beer from Unbarred to see what they are really capable of. And it's good.

title: Bluebell
description: A refreshing change from hipster (D/T)IPAs. A classic bitter from a Brighton-based micro brewery. A little watery but some great flavours hiding below the surface. Looking forward to trying some more from these brewers.

title: Let's Go
description: This IPA had a great first few sips when it was straight out the fridge but as the beer went on I fell slightly out of love with it. Can imagine it would be better suited drunk quickly and ice cold - not slowly over a warm evening (sorry I didn't do it justice). Only With Love are a small local brewery that seem to be popping up more and more. Will keep an eye out for more of their beers as this seemed promising but didn't quite work for me.

title: Locals V2
description: This is it, the 500th beer. Who would have thought that a 24-beer Ale advent calendar would turn into this? @thebeakbrewery are a brewery I have only recently discovered. Hailing from not-too-far Lewes, they brew consistently high quality beers - I wanted to be this that took the spot.

title: 42|DDH Pale Ale
description: Crisp, light and fresh Pale Ale with the added pizzazz of being double dry hopped. This might be the taste of cold beer after a long hot day but this was damn refreshing. This little number was part of the @brewdogofficial and friends box I got for free as a trial. Smashing work @brewbynumbers

title: Mr. President
description: I was quite excited to see a small @brewdog DIPA as it seemed to tick all my boxes, however the taste did not live up to the hype. First sip felt promising, but it was swiftly followed by a flat flavour with a slightly chemical aftertaste. Such a shame

title: Iskold Classic
description: This was not the colour I was expecting this "lager" to be. Decent taste and certainly drinkable, just nothing outstanding

title: Session IPA
description: I don't normally go for "Session IPAs" as can be a bit bland. This ale from @toastale, however, was full of flavour and delightfully morish. It was a shame to see the end of it. Toast Ales are made from surplus bread and all their profits go to charity - so not only did this beer taste great, I felt morally better by drinking it. A little present from my mum

title: Order of the Faith
description: A collaboration between @northernmonk and @alphadeltabeerand part of the "Twist on Faith" series. A classic hazy DIPA which was fruity and smooth, easy drinking and a delight.

title: 18.05 Sandy Kerr // North Sea Sessions
description: An IPA/Lager fushion (IPL), I wasn't hugely impressed with this Northern Monk beer brewed with Donzoko Brewing Company. It tasted like a plain hoppy lager. Not for me

title: Zia
description: What a tough beer to rate. A 9.5% Tiramisu Pastry stout which was as sickly as it sounds. 440ml is too much of this beer - even 330ml is too big. First few sips of this were promising; you could smell the coffee without getting too close. The flavours were sweet and the kind of thing I was after yesterday evening after a meal out. I opened this at 8pm, and by 10:30pm I had only made it two thirds of the way through the glass - with some still left in the can. Very heavy, very thick and very sweet. Picked up at Tesco

title: Ikat
description: My first foray into @saltbeerfactory (having heard plenty of good things). This very juicy DDH IPA was nice enough, but nothing I would buy again. Kind of left a dry taste in my mouth but pleasant nonetheless.

title: Camden Pale
description: A fairly plain, non-descript pale

title: Written in the Dust
description: Since my first drink of theirs, I've always held @deyabrewery with high regard. Looking back, however, I realise I have only ever had one or their beers. However, my trust was not misguided as this was another good one 👌. It was a lush, thick creamy DIPA. Perfect for an evening in (I could imagine it being quite filling if you decided to have a second).

title: Parade
description: I've swiftly become a big fan of @thebeakbrewery beers (need to head over there for a pint soon) and this one was no exception. Picked up from @palate.bottleshop a couple of weeks back, this IPA was smooth and fruity and very easy drinking. Had to pace myself as I found I was drinking it extremely quickly...

title: For Your Eyes Only
description: This was a tasty DDH IPA which went down without incident. Nothing groundbreaking but a pleasurable drink.

title: Diceman
description: How can a brewery which gets IPAs and, in fact, every other type of beer so right get a stout so wrong. I could just about taste the chocolate coffee notes that are so often found in a stout below the slightly watery flavour. Didn't really enjoy this beer at all, which is a shame really. I did manage to finish it though.

title: DIPA
description: After the last few @unbarredbrewery beers I was excited about this DIPA offering. Unfortunately, it didn't live up to my expectations. I know this is an odd comment for an 8% beer, but you could taste too much of the alcohol. Strong and unforgiving - I think I'll leave this one on the shelf next time.

title: Don't Mess with Yorkshire
description: A roast dinner calls for a real ale, a bitter, and old man beer. This is exactly what accompanied my Bank Holiday Monday roast - @northernmonk's answer to the ale. It tasted like a good ale and certainly worked well with the roast potatoes. It had good body and colour and I would certainly drink it again.

title: Q.E.D Session Pale
description: I wasn't much of a fan of this one. Reminded me very much of a lager. I didn't think there was much taste there. It improved when my curry turned up as it complimented it well, but it was missing something.

title: Huddle
description: A lovely IPA from @gipsyhillbrew, given to me by @laurzstreet. Super tasty and easy drinking. Gipsy Hill consistently do excellent drinks and this is no exception.

title: Lupuoid
description: A tasty IPA from @beavertownbeer picked up from Sainsbury's. Easy drinking with plenty of flavour - would happily add this to my rotation.

title: Divine Faith
description: I'm a huge fan of the original Faith and have come to love DIPAs of late, so when I saw @northernmonk release a DIPA of Faith I had to order it (side note, very impressed with Northern Monk's delivery service once again). This beer had the colour and consistency of apple juice, which was quite confusing at first. It's a thick, full beer which is not to be drunk quickly and lasted all evening. Full of flavour and a great end to the day. It's not as good as the Faith, but still a very good DIPA

title: Jet Stream
description: The final beer from my Brewdog Lockdown Survival Kit and what a disappointment to end on. A "transatlantic pale ale" which means it's gone around the world a d lost its flavour. It tasted like a poor, mass-produced lager.

title: Jagged Edge
description: I didn't really enjoy this beer at all. I was hoping for more of a jackhammer feel, but it ended up more dry and harsh. Will definitely be avoiding this one in the future (although I did finish it)

title: New England IPA
description: Bought this one from Aldi a couple of days ago. Super smooth drinking and, for the price is a very good NEIPA. It's never going to be as good as one of the small brewery New England IPAs, but you can't ignore that it costs a third of the price. Will happily drink this again (and will pick a few more up next time I'm in store) There is certainly a "mass produced" aftertaste to it, but it's not enough to put me off. The best 10 4 Brewing (Aldi's own) beer I've had

title: Casual Pale
description: I've had a few @unbarredbrewery beers now, most of them being the "novelty" ones, so I picked this one up in @palate.bottleshop to see what a "normal" brew of theirs is like and, damn, it's smooth. Had this while preparing and eating the Sunday roast and it was spot on. Flavoursome and very easy drinking. Would easily have polished off a couple of pints of this.

title: St. Pierre
description: Easy drinking Belgian style beer from Aldi. Went very well shared with my dad and accompanying the Chinese takeaway on Friday evening. Not outstanding but a good sharing beer.

title: The Vermont Sessions
description: Brewdog and Northern Monk - can't go wrong, eh? Opened this with anticipation but when I took the first sip of this, I was convinced it wasn't that good. However,  as the drink went on, it got better and better to the point where I didn't want it to end. Smooth drinking and great taste. Remnants of a DIPA without being as strong. Picked this one up in Tesco.

title: Whitstable Bay Ruby Ale
description: Slightly disappointed with this as i thought I was a fan of the original (although never reviewed). It tasted a bit cheap and left an acidic taste in your mouth.

title: Bumpy Road
description: Damn, @thebeakbrewery don't struggle to make tasty IPAs do they?! This DIPA from them and @thornbridge smashes it out the park. Full bodied and full of flavour. Thoroughly enjoyed this whole can. If I had one cristisim, then the alcohol taste was a bit too present, but it wouldn't stop me buying this 8% monster again.

title: Lock Down
description: If you've been following along with my Brewdog exploration, it will come of no surprise to you that I didn't particularly enjoy this "Guava and Grapefruit Pilsner". Hardly any flavour at all - a weak lager with some fruit mixed in. I'm also not a fan of Brewdog having 3 beers called Lock Down which are all different. 🤷‍♂️

title: Golden Beer
description: I've seen the Amber bitter Banks's do in all the budget supermarkets and it's a great staple beer for 80p. This is the first time I've seen the golden offering from the brewery. Simplistic in taste and I much prefer the amber one. This one tastes like a £1 beer.

title: Wool
description: When I saw this collaboration between @thebeakbrewery and @northcraftbeer announced, I was very excited. Two of my favourite recently discovered breweries teaming up to make one of my favourite recently discovered types of beer? It was a definite must-buy, and what a great beer it turned out to be. Picked this up from @palate.bottleshop a few weeks ago as I saving it for something special. It's a lovely beer, but I feel like the price of it impacted on my enjoyment a bit. Despite this, I did still very much enjoy it. Full, thick, slightly fruity and hella strong. It's one of those beers that I'm glad I have tried it, but it won't be a regular in my basket.

title: Light Speed
description: The citrus in this beer is very prominent, both in smell and taste. It went wonderfully with the Chilli, cheese and nachos this evening - complimenting the Mexican food perfectly. I do fear, however, that this tainted my view of the beer. The remaining sips after dinner revealed a drink that was a bit too lemony for my taste and, despite being smooth to drink and a refreshing change, I wouldn't buy it again. Not bad though.

title: Hophead
description: This is a beer that isn't amazing, but one I could drink all evening of a session. It's hoppy and dry, with a classic bitter beer taste. It's definitely a "drink in the pub" kind of beer as it tastes like a rowdy atmosphere and raised voices.

title: Anytime IPA
description: Instead of chocolate this year, Ma and Pa bought me beer for Easter. I always feel extra disappointment when I'm not a fan of beer someone has bought me, but this one was a bit flat with its flavour. It was a light IPA that ticked all the boxes except the taste. Went down easy enough while chatting with friends on zoom but highly forgettable.

title: Northern Star
description: This porter was slightly disappointing and quite forgettable. It was too sweet to be a classic chocolate and/or coffee porter of breweries old, but not sweet enough to be a dessert beer like the recent Brewdog one. Drinkable, but I won't be going back for another one.

title: Barnard Castle Eye Test
description: Now this is what @brewdogofficial are good at. Smooth and tasty hazy IPAs with no gimmicks. No weird flavours or curveball antics this beer had a lovely taste that was wonderfully morish.

title: Lock Down
description: I've come to the conclusion that Brewdog should stick to brewing "normal beers". Their fruity and alternative beers are novel at best and just plain old disappointing most of the time. Once again, this was a light sweet beer I wouldn't buy again. This was one of the few that I wasn't glad I tried "for the experience", but I did finish it.

title: Juice Shack
description: I've been thinking about this "Tropical Milkshake IPA" all day, trying to work out what I thought about it. It certainly reminded me of a milkshake, and had a juicy taste to it but did I enjoy it? I'm still not sure. I drank the whole can, I didn't turn my nose up at all but I didn't crave for more once I'd finished. A bit like the "Cream Soda Pilsner" I had the other week, it seems like a novelty. I'm glad I experienced it, but I wouldn't do it again anytime soon.

title: Jack Hammer
description: Another day, another Brewdog. This beer is described on the can as a "ruthless IPA" and, while I wouldn't go so far as to call it ruthless, it was certainly more abrasive than the other IPAs from @brewdogofficial. A welcome change from the sea of pale ales in the Lock Down Survival Kit, this 7.2% beer was one that got better the more you drank it. I can almost say I _really_ enjoyed it by the time I got to the last sip.

title: Double Hazy
description: I've finally completed the triple. Finished the Hazy series. The double hopped version of @brewdogofficial's Hazy Jane (and the little brother of Triple Hazy), this beer has the best of both worlds. The kick that comes with a DIPA without the overbearing of alcohol found in the triple. This beer got better the more I drank it, and the 7.4% was a curve ball that snuck up on me. Would highly recommend trying the original first then moving onto this. Killer beer.

title: Beach Break
description: This was an odd one. I was expecting an old fashioned ale, but a pale ale with a lemon twist. Took me a few sips to get into it, but once I found the groove I was able to enjoy it. Definitely wouldn't have it again, but was palatable for the evening.

title: Duo-polis
description: Maybe it was a mistake have this one straight after the Mallow quest, but this beer was disappointing. I was hoping for a thicker beer (as it was an oat cream) but turned out to be a fizzy IPA with not much flavour.

title: Mallow Laser Quest
description: This was an unusual one to get used to and not really one to have with dinner (more of a post-dinner beer, really). A consistency that reminded me of my pineapple juice and lemonade days (with no fizz) combined with a slight after-taste of the mallow. Very sweet and the first beer (in over 450) that both my wife and I liked. Can't say I would buy this again, but enjoyed it nonetheless.

title: Anchor Steam Beer
description: A brew from 1896 (I assume the recipe, not the beer), this random beer was picked up in Asda last week. Crisp and inoffensive, it was the kind of beer you would expect to find in a Gastro pub or wine bar, when they want some "cool" token beers in. It had some flavour, but nothing to shout about.

title: Twice Tangled
description: With my recent frivolity into Double IPAs, this somewhat piqued my interest when I saw it on the Tesco website. Not only is it going back to my traditional beer-tasting routes with a classic ale, it has the modern twist of my recent flirtations. I've had this double hopped version of Badger's Tanglefoot in the fridge for a couple of weeks - was saving it as an accompaniment to a roast dinner (to which it was a great match). There was some of the DIPA characteristics there, but not enough for my liking. It was more of an intense flavour of a beer which didn't need it. Nice enough, but I think I'll stick to the classic Tanglefoot if I'm feeling that way inclined.

title: Layer cake
description: This is a dessert stout if ever I had one. Marshmallow and chocolate and you can taste it all. Reminds me of a beer flavoured marshmallow rather than the other way round. A thick stout which is definitely best enjoyed over an evening. Probably not with a big heavy dinner as it's going to fill you up. You can't really taste the 7% kick until you go to standup. I did enjoy it though, went very well with film night

title: Model Citizen
description: @brewdog Vs @saltbrewing - this tasty NEIPA went down well with my pizza. Slightly thicker than your average IPA which made it a slow sipper - which is just what I was after. Nice taste too

title: Fake Empire
description: This beer was as disappointing as I was expecting. I don't tend to buy "sour" beers but this came in the Lockdown kit from Brewdog, so I had to try it. First taste is watery, followed by the sour hit. After swallowing, you get a hint of a "cool" breath - much like an Airwaves chewing gum. It got better as it went on but I still couldn't finish it. This beer did, however, confirm that my non-drinking wife does find sour beers "ok".

title: Lock Down
description: An interesting beer from @brewdogofficial. Thought I would go straight out the gates from the Lockdown Survival Kit with this Lock Down beer (their inconsistent spelling of Lockdown irks me). This "cream soda pilsner" is definitely a novelty drink. It's a "I drank it for the experience" kind of drink. I definitely wouldn't buy it again and I would probably say no if offered one. It tastes very much like cream soda, took me back to my childhood, with an alcoholic twist. Would recommend for the nostalgia but that's about it.

title: Stars and Stripes
description: I think this is the first sub-par Northern Monk beer I've tried. It's part of their "twist" series, so I won't hold it against them. A PB&J porter, the only taste I got was a slight jammy sweetness in the aftertaste. Wasn't too heavy or sickly though

title: Lost
description: 2 months ago Brewdog ran a campaign whereby they promised everyone in the UK could get free beer. A 4 pack of their Lost Lager finally rocked up yesterday, so I proper battle tested it this evening with 2 cans. Not really an evening sipping beer, but certainly did the job. One of the few lagers I would happily opt for over some of the other beers I've reviewed. Light and crisp but with plenty of flavour.

title: Bishops Finger
description: I can't believe I'm 448 beers and over 4 years into this review malarkey and I've only just got round to posting a Bishops Finger. No, it's not from some 2 person hipster garage and yes, it is available in every kind of shop with an alcohol license. But, as I was drinking this this evening, I was reminded about how good a beer it is. Full bodied, morish and a good old classic ale. I'd not bought one in a while as, for some reason, my memory of it was not favourable, but it's a very tasty solid pint.

title: Levy
description: Had this sitting in my fridge for a while as I was reluctant to drink it. I've had it a few times before and always been unimpressed. It's trying to be a black IPA (or a "light" stout) but falls short of the mark. There are some coffee/chocolate notes there but they get overwhelmed by the watery taste.

title: Summer Pale Ale
description: Yes, I know it isn't summer yet but in our local Co-op you have to queue past the beer so I picked this up on a whim. Light and easy drinking, would be good in a garden on a hot day.

title: Arise
description: When I went into @palate.bottleshop a couple of weeks ago, I asked for the owners favourite beer. After a minutes pause she rattled off plenty of options and settled on Arise, saying it was one of their most popular beers and always sold out. I can see why it's popular, and it reminded me a lot of Hophead by Dark Star. It's a bit too much of a hoppy beer for me. I can tell this is just personal taste and if I was into light hoppy, pilsner beers it would be top dog.

title: Joosy
description: This juicy IPA hit the spot for Friday game night. Light & easy drinking. However, I felt like it was missing something. Took me most of the can to work out that it could have done with the DIPA kick at the end. It was talking the talk on the taste buds but failed to walk the walk in the aftertaste. A nice enough beer (tasty) but it wouldn't be at the top of my list to buy again.

title: Steyning Stinker
description: This smoked beer wasn't for me. It took me two-thirds of a pint to get to a point where I was actually "enjoying". This is only my second smoked beer, but the thicker, syrupy texture of the past one suited the smoked taste better

title: Salted Caramel Chocolate Donut
description: This @unbarredbrewery beer grew on me. The first few sips came after the mead, so my mouth wasn't quite sure what to do with all that information, but as the drink went on it got better and better. Mrs Ale House provided me with a brownie and caramel ice cream halfway through this can which paired nicely - it's not often you find a beer that works well with dessert. A bit too much of a "salt" taste (I know it's salted caramel) for my liking but still pleasurable.

title: Beeding Mead
description: I feel like this review is more for Mead in general than specifically this one. I've never had mead before so thought I would take the opportunity when I ordered my recent batch of beers from @riverside_brewery. It's very fizzy, very sweet and very light. Would happily have it as a prosecco/champagne/toast alternative drink at an occasion, but not one for drinking at home in front of the TV really. Reminded me of Appletise.

title: Tubbers' Tipple
description: Another good beer from @riverside_brewery. Lovely tasting ale, only slightly let down by the slight after taste of liquorice, which is not to my personal liking (to which it loses a half point). Looking forward to the second bottle of this I've still got in the fridge

title: Locals
description: Yet another incredible small brewery IPA. Not too far from me in Lewes, this refreshing, very drinkable beer hit all the spots. I was disappointed when I finished it. I'd not heard about @thebeakbrewery until just over a week ago when their beers start flooding my feed, so when I was in @palate.bottleshop last week I picked one up on a whim, and what an absolute corker it turned out to be.

title: Honeycomb Milkshake
description: How the hell did they make this beer taste like a milkshake?! The first few sips threw me right into a 'shake flashback. Once my taste-buds had settled, this wonderfully easy-drinking beer ticked a lot of my boxes. Despite being great tasting, I wouldn't add it to my regular "buy again" list. It's a one-off beer, a birthday treat. Certainly not one for everyday. A great beer though 🐝

title: Love Bug
description: What a fantastic beer. Darker than I was expecting but full of flavour. Thoroughly enjoyed this and was disappointed when I finished. Don't often award half points but this gets one 👍. Great work @cellarheadbrew and @onlywith_love and shout out to @palate.bottleshop for stocking.

title: Tangmere Tower
description: This was another one I had two of, both tasting wildly different. The first one was a 4 or 5, but I enjoyed this one a lot more. Made me feel like I was having my nachos in a country pub. A classic ale

title: Old Peculiar
description: A lovely full bodied old ale which has a hint of sweetness. Was a perfect way of ending an evening and hit the spot as a post-dinner tipple. This was another beer that I've had countless times but never reviewed!

title: Rambling Monarch
description: Had this the other day and it was the best one so far. The second one I had the following day was not so good, so basin g my review on the first. Fresh, bitter and a good ale.

title: Sneaky Steamer
description: A bit too grapefruity for me, I finished the drink but wouldn't rush to have another one of these. Despite that, I can't say enough how much these beers belong in a rustic pub or at a beer festival and how much I'm enjoying the comfort.

title: Beeding Best
description: Second beer from @riverside_brewery and one that is a lot clearer, and comes with a good solid taste. I think it is taking my taste-buds a bit of time to adjust from the smooth hipster craft beers to the rugged ales. Ordering from Riverside is certainly a comforting taste excursion; taking me back to "old man" pubs with friends, sitting by open fires in the winter. Lovely flavours and easy to drink. A bit of a bitter tang after each sip.

title: Dirty Arthur
description: Beer delivery day means beer on a Tuesday. This one tastes exactly how you imagine a beer festival to taste. Slightly cloudy (putting that down to not having time to settle) but an enjoyable pint nonetheless. Taking a sip of this transports you to days of yonder, where you would choose an ale from a stained leaflet purely based on the name, stagger up to beer belly tubbed man and ask for some in your glass. Not quite suited to home drinking, but a good nostalgia trip. @riverside_brewery

title: Proper Job
description: Another absolute banger of a classic I've not reviewed before. A crisp, golden ale and a great bitter. They've done a proper job with this one.

title: Saucery
description: This beer was an IPA and just that. It was drinkable (it didn't make me turn my nose up). I wouldn't rush out and buy it again, but if I was round someone's house (ha, I wish) I would not turn one down.

title: London Pride
description: I realised two things this week. First, my feed has become very craft-beery. This is mainly because of the readily available craft tipples in lockdown - it seems they swiftly adapted to postal pints. This isn't very good for an "Ale" review Instagram. The second thing I've realised was that I have never reviewed a lot of classic beers, like this one. Tasty, easy drinking, never disappointing

title: IPA
description: I actually bought one of these when I picked up the Pale Ale of theirs back in December, but completely forgot to post it. Of course, it meant I had to buy another one so I could remember what it was like. Light, fruity, refreshing and very easily drinkable. Smashing work @wolfoxcoffee (also meant I could buy some coffee when ordering, too)

title: Triple Hazy
description: The triple hopped version of the Hazy Jane I had a couple of weeks ago. This triple IPA packs an absolute punch at 9.5%. Certainly not one to be chugged but after a monotonous Friday, it was a great end. Felt lightheaded after a third but had a great taste with no chemical after-burn which you might expect from a beer of this ABV.  It's a great tasting beer but don't think I would queue them up very often - maybe once in a while as a treat. There is a Double Hazy, which might be a perfect combo.

title: Scafell
description: Home. A @northernmonk Beer in a Northern Monk class. Had to keep reminding myself not to chug this 6.5% IPA, but it was tricky as it was so damn tasty. Full bodied and smooth, was an absolute treat for a Thursday night. I'm sure this will be sneaking into the online shopping basket again. The Monks have smashed another one out the park.

title: Schlenkerla Marzen
description: The first bite (and yes it was a bite, not a sip), my mouth was assailed with the taste of smokey meat and a light porter. It was reminiscent of an Italian antipasti - some peppery/smoked thin slices of sausage served with a complex beer all within a glass. After a while, the novelty wore off and the last third was a drag to finish (would have been better as a 330ml). It also, oddly, made my tongue feel a bit numb. Definitely wouldn't have it again, gets a bonus point for unusualness.

title: Shipping out of Boston
description: A lager that's this colour?! And a lager that tastes good?! Wonderfully light and lager-like with a decent flavour to boot. Went very well with the takeaway curry I had this evening, but don't think it I would have enjoyed it as much if I was drinking it solo. I do, however, wish all lagers tasted like this. Despite the good review, it is still a lager and still has lager qualities, so can't get max marks from me. If this was a lager review account instead though, then well, I'd have bigger problems to worry about (like why I'm running a lager review account 😉).

title: Whitbier
description: While Wolfox do excellent coffee, this beer left a lot to be desired. A bit too clear for a wheat beer and had a zingy aftertaste. Not to my liking, but went down all the same.

title: Life in the clouds
description: A double dry hopped IPA to see in my Thursday evening. First sip was a bit of a disappointment but as it went on, it got a lot better - so much so I didn't want to finish it! There is a bit of a strong hoppy aftertaste, which knocks it down a point but still a pretty solid pint.

title: Saison Van Du Bruwer
description: A subtle lemony summer drink which is going down quite well after a Sunday roast. Would be better on a warm evening in the garden, but in the lounge will suffice. A Saison, I feel, needs to be drunk from an "elegant" glass and this @maltusfaber one is one of my faves.

title: Lucky Jack
description: A simple IPA which neither offends nor compliments the palate. Easy drinking, without a desire to drink more. Could a beer be any more mediocre?

title: Bloodhound
description: A massively tasteful red IPA. Packed with flavour and an incredibly smooth drink. Would definitely drink again

title: Kölsch
description: Very lager-like. Light and refreshing after the slightly heavier IIPA before. Managed to drink this one pretty quickly, so can't have been too offensive. Apparently true Kölsch beers are like champagne in that it can only get the name if it is brewed in the region of Cologne

title: Softly Spoken Magic Spells
description: Beer 2 of the Zoom tasting. A lot easier on the taste-buds & a lot easier to drink (dangerous at 8.6%). The orange comes through in both taste and smell but isn't overpowering. Bordering on the enjoyment of a DIPA - would pick another one of these up if I saw it.

title: Oude Geuze
description: Beer one of the zoom tasting evening with @laurzstreet. We both ordered the January mystery mix box from @beermerchants. Started with this one which has the most dubious description (to be served with Apple and Vinaigrette pudding?). This instantly took me back to a summer evening on Brighton beach. I wouldn't order it again, but not as bad as I was expecting. Very Belgian in taste, but not a premium flavour.

title: Project Babylon
description: Last of the @gunbrewery Christmas beers from Mrs Ale House. Orange hints with a bog standard IPA. This is a Rod Stewart of the Gun Brewery range. Inoffensive but nothing spectacular

title: Natur Radler
description: As per Ale House lockdown rules, when a beer delivery turns up, one must be drunk that evening. As it's only a Monday, I picked this 2.5% Radler (shandy) from the lot. It's exactly as you would expect from looking at the can: a slightly alcoholic lemonade & does not taste at all like a beer (so much so my beer-hating wife said "it was nice") so I'm tentative about its appearance on this feed. Nice thirst quenching Monday night drink

title: How's the Serenity?
description: This caught me off guard. The lightness and "fruity" description had me wary, but it was very crisp and refreshing and I felt like I had to savour every sip. One of my favourite @gunbrewery beers

title: Neck Oil
description: If someone was "getting into beers" and asked for a gateway hipster craft beer, I would highly recommend this. Very easy drinking, mellow flavours and doesn't really taste like "beer" per se. Nice enough, not as good as Gamma Ray though

title: Prospect
description: It's good to have a pint of bitter again. Got a bit shaken up in a backpack walking home but was an excellent beer nonetheless. Hepworth are excellent local brewers - I need to hunt some more down

title: Far Fetched
description: An enjoyable IPA. Can't say much about it, good or bad, but I generally enjoyed it.

title: Red IPA
description: This red IPA was full bodied and had a lot more taste than the API before it. A good beer which is more suited at home, that for sure. Enjoyed while putting the world to rights over a video call.

title: Brighton Tea Party
description: A gentle API, which could be easily drunk pint after pint in a pub of an evening, but not really home drinking. Had a bit of an odd aftertaste pang and wasn't as full bodied as I would have liked

title: Atlantic Pale Ale
description: I feel like Sharps are one of those bands that do plenty of excellent songs but are only known for their most popular hit (which in this case is Doombar). It's a shame as they produce some very tasty beers indeed, like this one. Crisp, clean and easy drinking.

title: Laid Back IPA
description: A non-exciting IPA to fill the shelves of a supermarket. Brewed especially for M&S and it can stay that way, out of the mainstream. A filler beer really, drinkable(with some taste) but just about that.

title: Parabellum
description: A lovely stout for post zoom New Year festivities but pre-midnight celebrations. Went down. Ice and smoothly. Nothing too heavy after an evening of stuffing my face.

title: Numb Angel
description: A lager from @gunbrewery and, in my opinion, their first "failure". Lacking taste and character. Was refreshing with a Pad Thai last night but that was it. Won't be having this again.

title: Chummy Buster
description: This canned best bitter tasted like it should have come from a bottle. A fantastic full-bodied ale which went down exceedingly well with a stew. I was disappointed when I finished it and even more disappointed when I realised I didn't have anymore. @gunbrewery

title: Crème Brûlée
description: One of the great things about running a beer review Instagram is that people buy you new beers. I prefer a stout after dinner, and this was a perfect dessert. Had a sweet aftertaste which wasn't too sickly nor was it too heavy. The drinkability of this beer was very moreish and, although I don't often buy stouts, I will keep an eye out for this beauty. Good work @darkstarbrewco

title: Scaramanga
description: Back on the @gunbrewery beer and this one is a slow grower. After the first few sips I was sure it was going to be a 3, but the more I drank the slightly better it got. Was expecting it to be more of a sour but was just sharp and citrus-y

title: Lava
description: A coffee bitter, was a lot lighter when pouring than expected. I'm more used to a stout when it comes to coffee beer. Not an overly strong coffee flavour, which is nice, a pleasant Christmas evening sipper. Not sure I would buy it again, though.  Shout out again to Sister Ale House for the donation.

title: Filungar
description: Merry Christmas! The first of many festive beers, no doubt and an "off brand" photo (sorry). Provided by Sister Ale House, Gipsy Hill are becoming one of my favourite "curve ball" breweries. Lovely beer (could have done with the whole can but shared with Pops Ale House). Crisp, flavourful and morish. Scrapes in at an 8 - might need to find some more to be sure

title: Spin Drift
description: My first foray into @gunbrewery beers and a good one to start. Full of flavour and a nice light IPA to kick things off, this went down very well for Christmas Eve. Although as 7, this could very easily be an 8 if I was in the right mood or it was sunny and warm!

title: Olympia
description: Picked this up thinking it was an old faithful I'd reviewed many moons ago, but a quick check of the index (link in bio) revealed this one was missing. I've had many a pint of this and it never fails to tick the boxes. Not an outstanding beer, but I've never had a bad pint. Cheers @harveysbrewery!

title: Hazy Jane
description: I had started to give up on @brewdogofficial as anything more than an "ok" brewery, but these last two beers are starting to make me come round to them. Maybe it's just their mainstream "available everywhere" beers are their most average. This was a corker and I could drink this for many hours.

title: Double Punk
description: After seeing this double IPA on my feed, I felt like I had to try it. I've been a big fan of DIPAs recently, but this is the worst one yet (however, is it still better than a normal Punk IPA). Over 8%, and I felt it, there was an unusual post sip-twang I want keen on. I would still definitely have another one of these if offered.

title: Amber Ale
description: An attempt at a classic beer from Harper's (Aldi's own brewery). It's got the body of a bitter, but the after taste of an Aldi beer.

title: Pale Ale
description: Picked up this at the train station on Friday after a week in the office. Wasn't expecting much from @wolfoxcoffee (being, what looks like, primarily coffee roasters) but this little gem was tasty. Crisp with a clean finish. Perfect as a train or desk beer I can imagine, but also pretty good with a pizza.

title: Golden Torch
description: A classic disappointing Aldi beer. Flavourless and bland.

title: Anti Establishment IPA
description: Well, this one caught me by surprise. The catalyst for the Ald IPA I reviewed a few weeks ago, I was not expecting great things from this, based on the fact I am neither a fan of Pink IPA nor their IPA love-child. However, this was darn tasty. Crisp, flavoursome and a cut above the others. This is want Punk should taste like and I will definitely be hunting more of these down

title: India Pale Lager
description: Well this is a disappointing beer. When you say lager, I think of this kind of taste. Bubbly, flavourless and a struggle to finish.

title: The Hop Stepper
description: Finally, an Aldi beer I can get on board with. Darker than I was expecting, but a lovely refreshing beer not too hoppy at all. I'll be picking a few of these up next time I'm in there.

title: Faked Alaska
description: When we were in Suffolk, I asked the gentleman working in @hopstersipswich what his favourite beer was. This was it, a "pudding IPA". It was an odd taste to begin with (and, unsurprisingly didn't go with my dinner) but once I got into it I started to really enjoy it. Wouldn't rush for it again, but was certainly as experience I'm glad I had. Had a bit of yeast in the can, which I wasn't expecting

title: Kobolo
description: Last of the Suffolk beers, this @adnams beer is one of taste. Opened after the disaster of the previous beer, this lager (yes lager) was a refreshing change. Light, fizzy and flavoursome

title: Gose to Hollywood
description: Mrs. Ale House picked me this up in M&S and what a disappointment. It reminds me of the "Tart" I had a couple of years ago. It tastes like a cheap orange Fanta knock-off. Had to go down the drain but, as with the tart, it gets a bonus point for Mrs. Ale House liking it

title: Briar Pilsner
description: A lovely full bodied, crisp pilsner which went well with the homemade Mexican meal yesterday. Wished I'd picked up a few more beers from @briarbankbrewery when I was in Suffolk.

title: Chuggernaut
description: Hazy session IPA cracked open to put a drop in the Toad in the Hole this evening. Nothing special, tasted like I should be in a trendybar with some "bros" talking loudly nearby

title: Great Glacial Lake
description: I've yet to find a bad DIPA. Still making my way through the Suffolk beers, this beauty from the same brewery of the DIPA I had the other day (@burntmillbrewery) ticks every box. Might have to see if they deliver down south!

title: New England IPA
description: I was starting to give up on Adams canned beer, but then this beauty came along. Crisp, full of flavour and lovely a pleasure to drink. Was disappointed when this one ended! Last of the Adams small cans and a cracker to finish on.

title: Mosaic Pale Ale
description: Another disappointing can from Adams, picked up while in their hometown of Southwold a couple of weeks ago. Once again, a forgettable taste which is such a shame. A smidge better than the last.

title: Strange Seas
description: This collaboration with Beavertown was plain. A sub-par IPA really - I expected a better beer from these two breweries. Like a combo DVD/Video player - It doesn't make it twice as good.

title: ALD IPA
description: There is an excellent story behind this beer, you should go and look it up - I've had my eye on it since it was released and I was very glad a long time friend of Ale House Rock had a can spare for me to try. It's a shame the taste didn't quite live up to the legend. I suppose it isn't that much different from Punk IPA, so if that's your jam then maybe you'll like it.

title: Double Nelson Fog
description: Another Suffolk beer from holiday, this stonking double IPA was spot on for an evening sip. I've become a big fan of double IPAs after my tickle with Fourpure and this one from @burntmillbrewery ticks every box.

title: Whitstable Bay Organic Ale
description: Although the bottle says "Faversham Steam Brewery", I've tagged this under Shepherd Neame as they are known for the Whitstable Bay beers. Not as good as the original, a slight post-sip tang which I'm not too sure of. Picked this one up in co-op pre holiday (not knowing I would buy so much beer!)

title: Perpendicular
description: A holiday away in Suffolk calls for a sample of the local beers. Drove to, and waited outside, the @briarbankbrewery bar to open - only to find on their website they are closed at the moment. With a heavy heart we headed back to the car. On the way, we stumbled upon @hopstersipswich which fortunately stocked the beer (plus a few others coming soon). This beer is a classic British bitter. Was lovely to taste this bottle conditioned golden goodness. Worth waiting for

title: Golden Ale
description: I was starting to think I should just post all the beers I buy from Aldi as a 7 and be done with it, but this one bucked the trend. I've never had a beer that started off bad and got worse with each sip. An ambience which was remeniscent of the CBD beer I had before. Managed to choke down the last few mouthfuls. This is definitely not a beer I will be buying again.

title: Brut IPA
description: An interesting beer from Aldi. Very much trying to jump onto the hipster beer bandwagon and they do a pretty good job at holding on. Good flavours and easy drinking, just not as morish as the big boys. Will be keeping my eye out if ever I find myself there again.

title: New England IPA
description: Had this been 6 months ago, I wouldn't have been seen dead with a craft beer in my fridge. Lockdown, however, seems to have altered my perspective on such beer. This collaboration between @brewdog and @cloudwaterbrew is an absolute corker. Unexpected sediment at the end meant I couldn't quite get the last mouthful I was expecting, but good nonetheless. Perfect accompaniment to Tony Hawk's 1 & 2 on PS4

title: New Dawn
description: Good to be back on the bottled beers again after a stint of cans. Drank last week but I can still remember the fruity lightness this beer carried. Plenty of carbonation, so more of a summer tipple, but pleasingly tasty

title: King Slayer
description: After a months hiatus from posting new beers (don't worry, I was still drinking beer - I just fancied a few weeks of drinking some old favourites without having to think of scores), I thought I would get back on the wagon and post this beauty. I've had a few @buxtonbrewery beers in the past and some have been a bit meh, but this one is incredible. Smooth, very drinkable and a drinking 8%. Reminded me of a couple of Fourpure beers. Excellent stuff Buxton!

title: Striding Edge
description: My last of the @northernmonk beers from Mrs. Ale House. Didn't expect much from this 2.8% as the low ABV beers tend to lack some body. A tasty tipple, but nowhere near the absolute bangers they have otherwise produced. Takes a while to get into, but eventually becomes moreish.

title: Rock the Bit
description: This is the beer I was hoping the other one would be. Smooth, full of flavour and easy drinking. Could sink a few of these of an evening. Went down well with a game of Catan

title: Said & Done
description: I've seen plenty of @trackbrewingco beers floating around my timeline so I was excited to finally give one a go. Light, fruity, smooth and easy drinking. Certainly looking forward to trying more of their beers.

title: Slow Burn
description: I feel like I was deceived with this beer as nowhere on the can does it say it was a sour. I only found out when I googled and discovered it was a tweak from a previous sour brew. It tasted like fizzy orange and lemon squash. Managed to finish the whole drink (maybe egged on by the novelty of drinking out a new glass) but I definitely won't be buying this again.

title: USA
description: Getting a bit bored now of writing about mediocre beers from mediocre beer subscription services. I won't be buying from Flavourly again as there wasn't one beer I would eagerly buy again. This was the same, it was "nice" but there are plenty of other IPAs out there that absolutely smash this.

title: The Shy Retirer
description: Average micro pale at at 2.8%, it's a tidy beer for when you want nothing too strong. Bland taste but better than many other low alcohol beers.

title: Eternal
description: Another absolute corker from the @northernmonk team. Smashing it out of the park. Once my fridge is bordering on empty again I'll order some more - with a matching glass too. Fantastic flavour and horrifically moreish. Shame it was my last one!

title: Camden Hells
description: Not sure if this strictly belongs on an Ale review account, although this has now turned into a "beer in a vessel" kind of place. Rescued this from the desolate office the other week as it was due to go out of date soon. This lager was nice and I would happily drink again. Preferably in a garden or other outdoors place with it being chilled. Nice hoppy taste, easy sippin' and went down well.

title: Birds and Bees
description: Another "meh" beer from Flavourly. However tempting, I must never buy from them again. It's just a box of bland beers.

title: Revival
description: This beer from @drinkmoorbeer was in my latest @flavourlyhq box. There were 4 of them in the box and after 3, I'm still not sure what to make of it. It tastes like you've just eaten a sweet and had a swig of beer (sometimes), watery (sometimes) or like a fine craft beer (sometimes). I would drink again, but re-buyability is low.

title: New Zealand Ultra Pale
description: Such a non-descript beer. Reminded me a lot of the Octopus Energy craft lager, just slightly worse. It was so bland I'm struggling to write anything about it. Yet to find a @flavourlyhq beer which is top drawer

title: Salzburger
description: A "tasty" lager. Good depth and flavour but wouldn't actively buy again!

title: Dark Skies
description: This is an odd one and not a usual stout from @drygate (via @flavourlyhq). There was no initial shiver you get from a full-on flavoured stout and it wasn't the usual heavy taste. Somewhere there was a hint of coffee, but nothing overpowering. A good gentle sipper, which could be mixed in with a session, rather than being a nightcap

title: Monument Valley
description: First beer out of the @flavourlyhq box from this week is a @fourpure classic. A "tangerine pale", I was hoping for a Juicebox style beer but, instead, was faced with a pale ale with an orangey aftertaste. Palettable, and I'll happily drink the other 4 but certainly not one of my favourite Fourpure beers.

title: Black
description: This "seriously silky stout" was anything but. Had a bit of an odd fizz to it and was not a scratch on the porter from the other night. The bottle design also leaves a lot to be desired. Overall, not that impressed.

title: Pilsner
description: Sorry for all the new beer spam, @beer52hq delivered this week. This, hands down, is one of the best lagers (pilsners) I've ever had and would easily pick it over many of the ales I have previously drunk. Crisp, flavourful and absolutely smashed the role of being an accompaniment to a dinner of homemade chilli cheese and chips. Need to find where I can buy some more @sevenbro7hers beer

title: Market Porter
description: One of the best porters I've had. Strong coffee flavour which didn't ruin the taste. Easily sat and drank this one (normally have to slog my way through a porter) and was slightly disappointed when I finished it. Good work @thornbridge

title: Bevvie Across the Mersey
description: Such a long title for a sub-par beer. Every few sips I would think it was turning a corner, but I would just be disappointed by the follow-up. You could smell the citrus/pineapple, but that was the only enjoyable thing about it. Drinkable, but avoidable.

title: Kipling
description: This was such a forgettable beer. Tasted nice, but was not desirable in anyway. I drank it because it was there, not because I wanted more

title: Investor IPA
description: Another #latergram (need to get better at posting these when I drink them). Boring, classic IPA from my  @beer52hq box this week. Could drink a few of these and would definitely taste better if it was proper fridge cold.

title: Blonde
description: This is very much a #latergram. This light, crisp beer was perfect on one of those hot summer days we had last week. Would very much have one of these again

title: Faith
description: Now this. This is the epitome of the craft beer. An absolutely amazing pale ale and one I would drink time and time again. This isn't my first one of these and it definitely won't be my last. Fruity, but not overbearing, refreshing and wonderfully balanced. One of the best beers I've had to come out of a 330ml can. Bravo @northernmonk

title: East Coast Pale Ale
description: I had a hankering recently for some ale. Not craft beer in a can, but a bitter in a bottle and this beer from @bearislandbrews delivered. Sharp at first (maybe becuase I'm not used to the flavour) but by the end of the near-pint, I enjoyed it. Crisp and tasty, could drink a good few of these.

title: New World
description: Really loving @northernmonk at the moment. Absolute sterling lineup of beers. This one was classic craft IPA - hoppy and dry.

title: Oats of Disobedience
description: A creamy, oat stout from @peoplelikeusdk. Went well with a couple of squares of dark chocolate last night. I still don't think oat stouts are my thing but this was one of the better ones.

title: Rapscallion
description: A @northernmonk beer Mrs Ale House picked up in Tesco for me the other day. Not one of their best but still better than a lot of other beers out there. Light and hoppy with a fizzy tingle at the back of the throat. Slightly fruity but nothing to write home about.

title: Future Proof
description: @brewdogofficial Vs @moderntimesbeer. I appreciate the brewery above isn't "right" but wanted to group the Brewdog Vs. beers. Now about the beer... It's great to have a big beer again. It's only a few more ml (110 I think) but it makes a big difference in the longevity of a beer. This IPA was nice, but expensive. There are hints of fruit, it was smooth, had a lovely flavour and I could imagine drinking it on the beach or relaxing in a garden. 🏅 7/10

title: Unite Tribute IPA
description: I drank this IPA without incident. Was ok, went down well and was a little bit disappointed when it finished. I wouldn't reach for it again but wouldn't turn it down if I was offered one

title: Origin
description: Have seen a few @northernmonk beers cropping up on my timeline so thought I would give them a go. I have had a cheeky couple already and I can tell you they are spot on. This gluten free beer is wonderful. Crisp, light and, surprisingly, remenicent of a wheat beer. Northern Monk are definitely on my list of "breweries to buy from again"

title: Craft Lager
description: I won a crate of this beer in an @octopus_energy pub quiz. I've had a few Octopus Energy beers before, IPAs, and this is disappointing in comparison. Hardly any taste and fizzy. It's a good beer for when you fancy a drink but don't want to use up your good stuff

title: Dream Sequence
description: Excellent brewery name @yeastieboysaus! This American brown ale had notes of chocolate and orange. It was very smooth and went down well. Definitely an evening sipper. Might consider picking a couple of these up if I see them as an evening long drink.

title: Bibble
description: Now this. This is a @wildbeerco beer I can get behind. Lovely balance of flavour - refreshing and hoppy without being too overbearing. This is definitely one I would buy again if I saw it in the shop. Great way to end a somewhat interesting fling with Wild Beer

title: Take Up Space
description: The sound of this raspberry and vanilla stout drew me into this, however I was disappointed in the lack of those flavours. I'm sure a more sophisticated palate would have discovered them, but for my simple tastes I found it lacking. Such a shame. Smooth stout otherwise

title: Tea Party
description: Another beer with a twist from @peoplelikeusdk. This time a cream ale with green tea. Nothing outstanding about this beer - good flavours but as a non-green tea drinker I can't say I picked up on any vibes. Smooth to drink and went down well.

title: Pepper Spray
description: An interesting concept from @mikkellerbeer and @peoplelikeusdk. An IPA brewed with black pepper. First few sips were nothing special but once I got into the groove, and took a breather, the black pepper started to really come alive. Not overwhelming, but enough for you to notice in the aftertaste. Was spot on as an accompaniment to a pepperoni pizza. 👌 It's a tough one to score as I often rate beers based on taste and if I would buy it again. I'm glad I've had this, it was a nice taste but I can't say I would reach for it if I saw it on a shelf (except maybe for someone else)

title: Desert Haze
description: First beer out of the @beer52hq delivery was this lovely little number from @gipsyhillbrew. Light, fruity and a fantastic beer to accompany an end-of-the-day meeting. Would definitely pick this one up again if I saw it, although unfortunately it's a collab with the Beer52, so doubt it will be in any shops!

title: The Cranborne Poacher
description: Forgot to post this on Sunday. With my recent splurge on Craft beers (as those breweries tend to be the ones delivering in lockdown), I found myself down the beer aisle of my local supermarket yearning after a good old fashioned ale. Along with a couple of @hobgoblin_beer IPAs (Number 223, 9/10), I picked up this number from @badgerbeers. Reading the back of the bottle when I got home, it said it had notes of liquorice - something I'm not keen on myself and made me slightly regret my purchase. When drinking it, you could taste the sweetness which left an odd taste in the mouth. I managed to finish it but I wouldn't buy it again.

title: BrettBrett Double IPA
description: After the success of the last IPA I had from the @wildbeerco I was hoping this was going to be a tad better than the last few Wild Beers. Definitely stronger and with a bigger punch (another strong one at 8.4%), I manged to polish two bottles of this off this evening. Went well with the curry I had and was a decent pour (careful of the sediment at the bottom of the bottle). Solid taste.

title: Deucebox (A.K.A. Double Citrus IPA)
description: If there is a silver lining to this lockdown, it's my discovery of  @fourpure and their excellent delivery service. Once again, they've produced an absolutely cracking beer. Not quite as good as this one's little brother, Juicebox, but still an excellent drink. An 8.3% double IPA, this drink was a slow sipper last night but was a great way to end the week.

title: Wild Goose Chase
description: I don't think @wildbeerco is for me. Another day, another beer I didn't really enjoy. This was better than the last, but not a scratch on their IPA.  Oddly, every sip I took transported me back to Brighton beach, when I was 18 drinking beer from the @fortuneofwar

title: Modus Operandi
description: This was disappointing. Started off not realising it was bottle conditioned so ended up with a glass full of sediment (as you can see at the bottom). When taking a sip of this "oak aged" beer, I was assaulted with the abismal taste of red wine, and it stuck. If you are a fan of the red grape juice, I can imagine you rather liking this beer - but I can't stand the stuff so its a big no from me and the rest ended up down the drain as it was ruining my dinner. It does get an extra point as my wife, who has an adverse dislike for any beer, is contemplating drinking the second bottle I have of this. Sorry @wildbeerco

title: Star City
description: Followed up the last beer with this absolute beast of a near-pint from @fourpure. Brewed in partnership with @mothership.beer, this double IPA is an absolute stunner. Wouldn't want too many for these, but was perfect as an evening wind-down and I'm glad I've got a second one in the fridge. It was full of hops with a nice deep flavour, Fourpure have done it again.

title: Rift Valley
description: Despite being 2.8%, this micro pale packed a punch. There is no loss in taste with that low ABV and I'd be happy to drink these all night. A colour that looks like apple juice but a flavour that is fresh.

title: Madness IPA
description: A much better beer from @wildbeerco than yesterday's one. Many hops with a classic bitter taste (and a 6% ABV), this beer went down rather well. A bit too hoppy for my taste, but I appreciated a good beer

title: Sleeping Lemons Export
description: My Wild Beer delivery turned up yesterday and thought I would give them a crack. Bought this as it was one of @wildbeerco's most popular beers (well, this is the stronger Export version at 6%), but I struggled to see why. I believe this taste and cider-like balance is bordering on the "sour" trend which is popular at the moment, as it was rather lemony in flavour. Definitely a summer "garden party" beer - maybe one to be enjoyed while wearing a trilby and deck shoes. Not for me I'm afraid

title: Longboard
description: The carbonation on this beer is epic. That aside, this was the last of the 3 different @konabrewingco beers I had delivered the other week and I have mixed feelings about it. I ordered two and drank the first a week or so ago and at the time, I wasn't impressed. A bit meh. This one, on the other hand, was a bit better - it had more character and complimented my roast dinner quite well (surprisingly). Based on my findings, I will lean generously towards the latter experience with my rating. Still not as good as the "Big Wave" though

title: Costa Rican Coffee Extra Porter
description: For 7.4% I was expecting this beer to pack a punch - instead it floated like a butterfly and stung like a bee. In other words, you couldn't taste the high alcohol volume of this monster (although I had already had a few, so maybe it had less of an impact). Subtle flavours, but complimentary as a "dessert beer" to the ice cream I had late last night. Surprisingly tasty without being too heavy.

title: Gamma Ray
description: What a beautifully delightful beer. Was surprised about the excellent taste on this one as I was expecting Brewdog levels, but wow, it blew me away. Nice and light, full of flavour with a warm afterglow of "not a care in the world". Will definitely be buying more of these.

title: Lukas
description: After my last Thornbridge experience (Tart, 2/10) I was hesitant about this one. After a day in the garden, this helles style lager was perfect. Nice taste for a lager and a lovely looking can. Once the hard work of the day wore off, this turned out to be a better lager than most, but lacking anything significant. Would grab a couple of these for a BBQ but nothing more

title: Ocata
description: Was good to get a craft beer in a big can for a change. This IPA was lovely and smooth and went down very well while having a video chat with friends I'd normally find in a pub. It was missing "something" but I would certainly drink it again and could have drunk some more.

title: Black Ale
description: Forgot to post this from the other night. This beer was perfect after cooking and eating a Sunday roast and sitting down in front of the TV. Not too heavy and filling but was a full-on coffee flavour that took a few sips to get into. One of the better stouts/porters (black ales?!) I've had that hit the spot, and a perfect size too. 🏅7/10

title: Delta IPA
description: This 6.5% Saison IPA was a fruity little number. It got a bit better as the glass went on, but I still wouldn't buy it again. I have certainly had better Saisons!

title: Shelterstone
description: After a strong start out of the gates for Buxton the other day (from the @beer52hq delivery), I was looking forward to this one but was bitterly disappointed. Tangy, odd taste . Certainly not a beer I would go back to.

title: Hanalei
description: I had high hopes for this Island IPA after Big Wave (the golden ale from the same company scoring so highly) but, alas, it seemed to fall into the "generic fizzy hipster IPA" category. Not offensive enough for me to not drink the second bottle I have, but I won't be ordering anymore of these. My string of good beers had to end somewhere!

title: Lupulus X Simcoe IPA
description: My first foray into @buxtonbrewery  and what a refreshing beer it was too. Had to stop myself from drinking this in one - despite the hazy appearance, it was a light, crisp IPA. Looking forward to the other Buxton beers in my fridge!

title: Shapeshifter (A.K.A. IPA)
description: I seem to be on a string of IPAs at the moment, but when they are this good I am absolutely fine with that.  This one was an amazing way to end the weekend and was perfect served fridge cold. The taste was sensational and was well worth the wait. @fourpure have smashed it out the park with this one and I will be ordering more of these come payday 😚👌

title: Flowerpower Blonde
description: My last of the @loudshirtbeer beers from the delivery a couple of weeks ago and what an absolutely awesome beer to finish on. Slightly floral (but not overpowering) and beautifully light. Perfect as a post-gardening pint 👌

title: Basecamp (A.K.A. Lager)
description: For a lager, this had excellent taste. Refreshing, light and full of flavour. I feel like I'm going to have to order some more @fourpure beers after this.

title: Easy Peeler (A.K.A. Citrus Session IPA)
description: Another citrus IPA but not a scratch on the other one. This one was a bit too orangey for me

title: Hypnosis Pale
description: Sublime beer from @loudshirtbeer - their best yet! I seem to be having a string of surprisingly tasty beer. With Pale in the name, I wasn't holding out much hope but this pint is top drawer. 🏅9/10

title: Last Train
description: Was sitting down to watch some live comedy, so this beer seemed ripe for the occasion. Although a stout, it wasn't too heavy or syrupy. Took a few sips to get into but was fully onboard by the time it was finished. Glad it was 330ml though and not a 500ml bottle. Perfect amount 👍

title: Pale Ale
description: A beer donated by die-hard Ale House Rock fan @mblackburn21 (don't worry, all rules were observed). Brewdog is everywhere, but I've not really been keen. This beer wasn't mind-blowing but is certainly one of their better ones. Every other sip revealed some beauty but it was always followed by something odd. Would definitely have this again over some of their other beers

title: Juicebox (A.K.A. Citrus IPA)
description: I had written this off as previous citrus/fruit beers had left a lot to be desired. This, however, was one of the best beers of this type I have ever had. I was blown away by the lush taste and the very subtle citrus flavour. Good job @fourpure.

title: Psychedelic IPA
description: What a wonderful and light beer. It might have been the sunshine and sitting in the garden that tainted the taste of this bad boy, but thoroughly enjoyed this pint - even when I came inside! One of my favourite of all the @loudshirtbeer beers I've had to date

title: Hemisphere (A.K.A. Session IPA)
description: Fresh delivery of @fourpure beer today so, naturally, I had to try one. This session IPA was good, but I've had better. While eating my Mexican dinner this evening, it improved somewhat but as a "drinking" beer, it was ok

title: Ecstasy Stout
description: Nice sunny Easter Sunday in the garden followed by a roast dinner called for a stout (more of a winter beer but it needed drinking!). 6.6% and you can taste it. Strong coffee/chocolate flavours as expected from a stout but lovely taste

title: Euphoria Red
description: Next up from @loudshirtbeer is this tasty, well balanced red ale. A lovely beer that got better with every sip. Not too heavy either (which was perfect after a good Friday pottering!)

title: Bohemian Gold
description: With a delivery fresh off the truck (we're in lockdown, but it seems beer delivery is deemed "essential"), it would have been rude to wait until the weekend to try at least one of the beers from @loudshirtbeer (I can confirm the driver lived up to the company name). Lovely, light beer with a good flavour. Something I can't quite put my finger on keeps it just shy of an 8, but still a lovely tipple

title: Tropic Like it's Hop
description: Another @innisandgunn_ fruit flavoured beer. Doesn't taste as good as its name. I much prefer the whiskey/rum barrelled beer they do

title: Porter
description: I purposely left this @harveysbrewery beer for the big 300 as they seem to have dominated the tail-end of this century. This porter was pleasant, but not outstanding. Nice and light, for a porter and subtle flavours.

title: Stateside IPA
description: This can stay stateside to be honest. This was the kind of beer you would expect from Aldi for £1.50. A slight ambience of taste with a watery finish.

title: Mangoes on the run
description: This was surprisingly better than I expected. I'm not really a fan of fruit beers, but this one surprised me. I'm not sure I'd actively seek it out again, but I won't say no if offered at a friend's house (if I'm ever allowed around a friend's house again)

title: Dry Hopped Lager
description: A good tasting lager. Would hammer these at a BBQ or on a summer's day, even enjoyed it during a lockdown in my lounge! Good one @adnams

title: Wild Hop
description: Finally got a Harvey's glass to accompany all those @harveysbrewery beers I've been having. Lovely light beer, easy to drink. Would have been better in a beer garden with friends but, alas, current circumstances do not allow for that.

title: Phantom Pale Ale
description: Surprisingly pleasent for an Aldi beer. Refreshing after a hard afternoon in the garden. Can't write much as this was from last weekened and I forgot to post!

title: Robbie's Red
description: This beer was a bit of a rollercoaster. First sip was good, then the beer tasted really watery for most of the pint. Trouble was, the last two sips tasted good as well! So "ok" on the whole.

title: Sweet Sussex
description: Among the chaos it's still great to be able to sit down with a beer, especially one as pleasant as this one.  A stout, but a nice, easy drink, lovely flavoured one. Not too heavy or laden with coffee notes, this beer was just right 🏅 8/10

title: Heated Seats
description: This beer was a lot better than I anticipated. It had all the hallmarks of being a hipster-no-taste pale ale but this beer was full of flavour. The smell took me back to Copenhagen where @mikkellerbeer is rife. Lovely job

title: Copperwheat
description: Yes, yet another Harvey's beer. They draw you in as they recycle the bottles, so you have to keep going back. Not that impressed with this one, had a weird tang and was too bitter for me. I've definitely had better wheat beers.

title: Ghost Ship 0.5%
description: I was designated driver last night, so on the non-alcoholic stuff. This had a very metallic tang (possibly impaired by the Tanglefoot that preceded this). It got better with each sip and accompanied the curry I was eating ok, but I won't be buying anymore of these. 🏅 5/10

title: Happy Every After
description: This IPA tasted like it belonged at a beer festival. Bottle conditioned, this flat beer went down well with great flavours, although I was expecting better based on the recent IPAs I've had. Apparently this beer is a homage to Wills and Kate and also Harry and Meghan Sperkle 🤷‍♀️

title: High Wire
description: This beer was as disappointing as the hipster design of the can suggests. Left a dry taste in my mouth (had to have a glass of water) and was very hoppy. I'm sure Brewdog fans would enjoy this, but it's not for me. On a related note, if you ever want to search for a beer or brewery, I launched a website allowing you to do just that (link in bio)

title: Star of Eastbourne
description: What an incredible IPA. Full of flavour and a wonderful taste. It doesn't actually state it's an IPA but an East India Ale - can't say I could tell you the difference 🤷‍♀️. Lovely beer and a stonking 6.5%. With these recent ales, @harveysbrewery has gone from "yeah, alright" to a brewery I would actively seek out to buy 👏👏

title: Abbot Reserve
description: Remeniscent of  the "Old Speckled Hen", Abbot Reserve is more refined, alcoholic and better tasting than the original Abbot Ale. For general flavour it is an 8, but I fear this 6.5% has gone straight to my head

title: Klute West Coast IPA
description: The first half of this beer tasted good, married with a Thai curry they went together very well. However, in the cold hard light of post-dinner drinking, I realised how bland tasting this was. What a rollercoaster of emotions

title: Black Sheep Ale
description: This was one of those beers I was sure I'd reviewed already. A regular occurrence in my fridge and, once you've had a sip, I'm sure you would understand. Tasty, full-bodied and full of flavour. A definite "buy again"

title: Everyday Hero
description: As you can probably tell from the colour, this tasted like a very watered down beer. I can't even dislike the taste, as there wasn't much taste to dislike. Plain, thirst quenching beer with nothing notable about it at all. Like a magnolia wall.

title: Sussex Gold
description: Had a pint of this out of the barrel in  @thewatchmakersarms the other night which has tainted my opinion somewhat. This beer is glorious. Better out the barrel than the bottle, but the bottled product is still supreme. Crisp, refreshing & light (without being too Hoppy). In the pub, this was easily a 10, but the fizzyness of the bottle means it loses a point. Still a cracking good pint though

title: Tom Paine
description: If I had to pick a beer that was the epitome of an English Bitter, this would probably be it. When non ale drinkers think of a pint of ale, and are asked to describe it, they would ultimately be detailing this. A straight-to-the-point beer, this pint had no frilly flavours, no fancy aftertaste but the result is an excellent drink. Good work @harveysbrewery

title: Brel
description: A light, crisp Aldi Belgian style IPA. Lovely in flavour with a great aftertaste. Will look out for more of these

title: Plunged Orange Pale Ale
description: I try not to have prejudices with my beer tasting, but I couldn't help but approach this one with caution. With the first few sips I was proved right, however by the end the score had increased by a point. Still only

title: Hop Hunter Session IPA
description: I was pleasantly surprised by this one. Not holding out much hope, this Lidl own brand beer was impressive. Much more suited to a summer's day by the BBQ, easy drinking and refreshing. If they are still around in a few months I'll be stocking my fridge with these.

title: Kiss
description: Wasn't too impressed with this one. Nice flavor to begin with but an odd aftertaste and a smell I couldn't quite fathom. Not sure I'll be picking another one of these up when I head back to the shop

title: Armada Ale
description: Now this is a beer that plenty of breweries can learn from. I've always been a fan of Armada Ale from @harveysbrewery and drinking this reaffirmed why. Lovely crisp golden ale with a fantastic taste and a morish flavour. I only wished I could afford to drink this all the time!

title: American IPA
description: Generic ASDA IPA in a can, not a scratch on the Fuller's and Harvey's from posts past. Hipster nothingness with fizzy disappointment. Eventually found some taste at the end of the drink, but won't be buying this again

title: Bengal Lancer
description: This was a cracking pint of IPA. Bottle conditioned, it reminded me a lot of a Young's Beer. I found this tucked away in my local co-op, dusty and neglected. One of the best Fuller's beers I've had

title: Hazey Daze
description: Fruity, but classic hipster IPA. Nothing exciting

title: Bonfire Boy
description: The final bottle of this @harveysbrewery exploration. A wonderful Christmas present (all the Harvey's beers of late) and a pleasent one to end the sprint on. It seems overall the cans faired better than the bottles! This one was indeed smokey (as stated by the bottle) and well balanced. Not too heavy, but plenty of taste. A very good beer and a great way to end the Christmas break

title: Malt Brown
description: A tasty full-bodied brown ale. Malty, bitter and definitely a sipper. Drank this after dinner (can't imagine it complimenting many meals - except maybe a roast) while playing cards. Wonderful.

title: Wharf IPA
description: I tried to get back into the habit of posting beers as I drink them, as it seemed to be slipping of late. I couldn't do it with this one as I drank it too damn quickly. Fantastic IPA - one of the best I've ever had. More please!

title: Southdown Harvest
description: A lovely beer that I didn't even think about drinking more of. Great taste and balance, but not ground breaking

title: Tin Lizzie
description: An excellent Harvey's beer. After the recent run of 5s, I was dubious about this one, but I was proved wrong. I can see why this was award winning - lovely taste and not too heavy

title: Albion Ale
description: Can you tell I got a @harveysbrewery selection of beers for Christmas? A big standard bitter,  reminiscent of those odd breweries you get in Aldi. Not very remarkable

title: Gold Bier
description: Hoppy with a tangy aftertaste. Would drink again if offered, but wouldn't select above other beers

title: Georgian Dragon
description: At the in-laws for Christmas dinner and this beer accompanied it perfectly. The right amount of bitter (although couldn't taste the fruitcake) and a lovely ruby beer taste. This might be the Christmas cheer & food talking but was an excellent beer

title: Kew Gardens Honey Ale
description: A lovely dark, sweet beer. Normally a stout like this would have coffee or chocolate notes, but this @kewgardens tipple was laced with honey flavour, making it a lighter stout to drink

title: Brasserie De Noir Bière Millésimée
brewery: Brasserie Goudale - Les Brasseurs de Gayant
description: The bottle of this one was 100% the reason I picked it up. An Aldi special, the cork drew me in and the sound that accompanied it would not disappoint. Nice beer, unusual to get a 750ml bottle but was a nice novelty. Perfect for the situation or fancying a bit more but not wanting another full pint. Easy enough to drink & reasonably priced too

title: Kew Gardens Golden Ale
description: Seem to be on a bit of a run of new beers at the moment, must be the run up to Christmas (started this account 3 years ago!). This light crisp ale from local "Kew gardens brewery" was easy to drink and could taste the hint of honey from local bees

title: Wheat Pale Ale
description: This reminded me very much of a lager with taste. Not remarkable but happy to drink. It's a hipster pint from @thekernelbrewery, so was pricey. Yeast in the bottle prevented pouring the full pint

title: Winter Special
description: A Lidl special and a cracking one at that! Wasn't holding out much hope for this one, but was a lovely little warmer after a night time stroll round @wakehurst_botanic_garden. Very nice pint

title: Lighthouse
description: Crisp, light golden ale. Lovely flavours, not ground breaking, but very pleasant indeed

title: Explorer
description: Lovely light crisp ale I picked up from @adnamsnorwich while on holiday this weekend. Had to stop myself from buying the whole shop! Would love to have this again, becoming a massive fan of @adnams brewery. Beer went down very easily

title: Both Barrels - Oak Aged Broadside
