description: Wow, what a punch this beer has. Brewed in rum barrels, this beer took a 6.3% bottle of Broadside and ramped it up to 9%! It tasted like it had a full shot of rum in it, which warmed the cockles with every sip but took away the lovely taste of the broadside beer. Worth a try, but wouldn't rush to have it again

title: Red Rye Captain Pale Ale
description: A watery taste to the start of this, but soon got into the rhythm of it - was disappointed when it finished! Nice little tipple from Lidl, would drink again

title: Deep Dive Human
description: Another beer I wasn't holding out much hope for but dang it was good. Juicy, without too much fruit, a lovely balance of flavours and not too heavy in the stomach. Would easily have a couple more of these

title: Wimbledon Ale
description: Another M&S beer and what a delight. Definitely be reaching for one of these if in buying beer from Marks and Sparks anytime soon. Lovely taste, went down well

title: Double Hopped Citra IPA
description: One of the longer named beers I've had. From M&S, this was a reasonably priced good beer. Taste was nice, went down well, but nothing that blew me away.

title: Steady Rolling Man
description: Going through a bit of a hipster beer thing at the moment and this is no exception. Pouring it out, I thought @laurzstreet had picked up a cider for me instead as it is very apple-juicey in colour. I wasn't holding out much hope but I was very much proven wrong. An absolutely lovely beer, easy to drink and very moreish. This is definitely one of the best hipster pale ales I've had

title: Crunch
description: Well this was an experience I would rather forget. A peanut butter milk stout (apparently the best in the shop). Was excited about this as I love peanut butter & milk and I have a fondness of stouts. It was not good, but took me the whole glass (yes, I did finish it) to find out. It's a flavour I can't put into words. I'm glad I tried it (I think?), It hasn't put me off trying other peanut butter milk stouts (I think?) but I definitely won't have this one again (I know that for sure)

title: Big Wave
description: Light & tasty golden ale. Could easily drink this for hours (although not at the price I had to pay for it). Very drinkable and refreshing without being too hoppy. Lovely

title: Light Pale Ale (Small Beer)
description: Lovely light beer from @wiperandtrue brewery. Went down very quickly - smooth and tasty and at only 2.7% could have easily had quite a few!

title: Winter Ruby Ale
description: An unremarkable beer that has taken me over a week to post as I wasn't sure what to put about it. Drinkable, but not memorable

title: Beatnik
description: A hoppy brother to the previous beer, but not quite as tasty (although I'm not a fan of hoppy beers anyway) but would drink again

title: Hepcat
description: From a brewery in crystal palace, this hipster session ale was very fruity and went down easily after a Chinese takeaway, but not one I would really lust after

title: Burton Ale
description: Finally managed to wrangle another beer from the Saltdean brewery guys. This rich, deep ale was lovely. A great range of flavours but it's definitely a sipping beer of an evening - wouldn't want too many of these (nor could I manage it). One while watching Elementary was perfect

title: Sayzon
description: Wow what can I say about this tasty mass-produced Belgium-style beer? It's got all the characteristics of a true Belgian tipple, dangerously qwaffable while being higher ADV than your average ale. Lovely stuff

title: Shipyard American IPA
description: Classic IPA. Nothing to write home about but a stable classic. I would happily sit and drink this all evening for a session

title: Nanny State
description: Now this is a non-alcoholic beer I can get behind. No metallic after-taste and a reasonable flavour. A little bit watery but by far the best alcohol free beer I've had. Would even choose this over some of the ales I've had previously

title: Iconic
description: This was a tasty little number from Aldi. Will definitely keep an eye out for this one as I would love to drink this again

title: Punk AF
description: A request from a die-hard fan, although I wish they hadn't asked. A non-alcoholic beer from the kings of mainstream hipster. Tastes like any other disappointing non-alcoholic beer out there

title: Hop Commotion
description: This beer stank of strawberries so wasn't holding out much hope for it. A surprisingly pleasant taste. Subtle fruity notes with a hoppy body. Would drink again, I think

title: Lansdown
description: I've been a fan of Bath Ales for a while and this one has "fallen" into my trolley a few times. Good taste & wonderful session ale

title: Öl Mörk Lager
description: Dark lager beer from IKEA. This is incredible, and saying that about a lager and something from IKEA is rare. Great taste and very easy to drink (and only £1.79!). Shame I only picked up 2, I could drink a whole case

title: Easy Times IPA
description: Forgot to write this last week. Had this freebie from a sample at work and I wish I hadn't. 0% alcohol with 8mg of CBD. It had the chemical taste of a non-alcoholic beer with the aftertaste of someone smoking marijuana walking past and coughing outside. Terrible taste but somehow I did manage to finish it

title: Blood Red Sky
description: Beer aged in rum barrels. Lovely deep taste, definitely a sipper at the end of the evening rather than a session ale. Complex flavours

title: Tanglefoot
description: Not much to say on this. Light, crisp, satisfactory. Hits the spot when it aims to be a "good beer after a hard day". Would never crave one but would be tempted to buy more

title: IPA
description: Had this lovely pint from @hobgoblin_beer the other day, after the other award winning beer. 2018 IPA of the year and by golly it deserves it. I felt spoilt having two award winning beers in one day. Brilliant taste

title: Shere Drop
description: @camra_official's beer of the year and I can see why. Amazing taste, lovely colour, wonderfully light but full bodied. Such a shame when it ended. Highly recommended

title: Clockwork Tangerine
description: Sorry for the radio silence, not had the opportunity recently. Kicked it off again with this average beer. Slight citrus notes with a whole lot of nope. Wouldn't have it again, but managed to finish the bottle. On a positive note (for those that are that way inclined) I learnt that all but a handful of @brewdogofficial beers are Vegan

title: Offshore
description: Light crisp pilsner - great for a hot day. Remanicent of the Hop House lager in its taste

title: Triple Hop
description: Last of the Genovese beers. Very hoppy, but unlike the hoppy English beers, this one was still tasty. Not into the light hopped beers so much these days, but this one still had some lovely notes

title: Hoptical Illusion
description: A lager, but a pleasant one. Would have gone perfectly with the BBQ we had planned (which the rain put a stop to). Light and hoppy, relatable to Hop House 13, but with a better name

title: Brune
description: When I poured this last night, our guests had mixed feelings. It had the consistency (and ambience) of an iced coffee. Was smooth, but heavy - definitely a post-dinner drink. Not so sure about this one

title: Tippa IPA
description: Popped over to Genoa to pick up a few beers from Maltus Faber, a fantastic Genovese brewery I was introduced to at Christmas. This one is no exception, great taste and a lovely flavour

title: Ease Up I.P.A.
description: In my opinion, @adnams is up there with the best. They can seem to do no wrong with their beers. Always lovely tasting and this is no exception. Crisp, tasty and went down well

title: IPA
description: A surprisingly excellent beer from co-op. Lovely taste & balance and wonderfully morish. Will be keeping an eye out for this one again

title: Sussex Best
description: A good old fashioned English bitter from @harveysbrewery. Can't go wrong with this pint

title: Ghost Dance
description: went down without incident. Nothing to report really. Not horrible, not staggeringly tasty

title: Sucker Punch IPA
description: Brewed by @octopus_energy of all people. Lovely light beer, perfect for a summer BBQ. Slightly lacking in a strong flavour though

title: Lyme Gold
description: This beer had a crisp tang on first sip which immidiately put me off but the proceeding sips managed to clamber back some sort of respect. Still not enough to have me bowled over but better than some beers I've had

title: Dorset Knob
description: Underwhelming as a beer. Drinkable, with a good(ish) taste but could have been so much better

title: Better Bitter
description: If this is their better bitter I would hate to taste their normal one. Eggy smell and a bad aftertaste, this was not what I was hoping for from a small brewery. Disappointing, especially after that 10 last week

title: Brian
description: Oh wow. What an absolutely fantastic beer. So good! This bottle came wrapped in the bit of paper, which gave information and a beer token for a free half pint at the brewers shop. A small brewery but a incredibly tasty beer. One I'm sure Brian would have thoroughly enjoyed

title: Pride of Dartmoor
description: Finally, a good beer from this part of the world. Full of flavour and body and a fantastic way to end the day. Cracking pint from @black_tor_brewery

title: Break Water
description: Not much of an improvement from the previous beer of theirs that I tried (see #213). Bit watery and not quite the "full flavour" as promised on the bottle. It's a shame I don't do half points otherwise it would be a 5.5.

title: Drift Wood
description: A completely average beer. Not disappointing, nor exciting. Would not recommend nor dissuade someone from drinking this beer

title: Peedie Porter
description: A classic sipping Porter to while the evening away

title: Dark Munro
description: This mild had the taste of a stout with the consistency of a black IPA. Went down without incident really. Coffee/cocoa tones but not a stunner

title: Long story short
description: A "table beer" from @lostandgroundedbrewers which leaves a lot to be desired. Far too hoppy and tasteless for my liking. Managed to finish it, just, but with a little grimace after each sip

title: Check Ignition
description: A Porter which apparently has raspberry notes - although they were lacking. Finished the pint but was underwhelmed

title: Island Hopping
description: Tasty session ale but would leave it there. A bit too hoppy for home sipping but would be wonderful and right at home in a small, independent drinking establishment

title: Keller Pils
description: It's not often a lager appears on this feed, so when it does - take note. This one from @lostandgroundedbrewers is a wonderful lager drink. More like a happy ale, I could happily drink pints and pints of this of an evening in a bar - not really suited for watching a film

title: No rest for dancers
description: Small brewery (@lostandgroundedbrewers)  with a big taste. Lovely red ale that tastes like a brewery. Full of depth and flavour

title: Landlord
description: One of my dad's favourite beers, I believe, and a good one too. Timothy Taylor have done an excellent job with this one

title: Grand AM
description: unexpectedly bottle conditioned for an APA. A strong taste, sipped this over an evening despite being small

title: Pedigree
description: I was sure I had reviewed this one, but a quick search proved otherwise. It was better than I remember it tasting and would actively buy one after this lovely pint. Thoroughly enjoyed it

title: Old Man
description: This is how this beer makes you feel, like an old man. It makes you yearn for a log fire and a cardigan. Full of flavour but not overpowering like some stouts. A long drink, one to be had over an evening and a conversation about what is wrong with the world

title: American Pale Ale
description: A slightly better Long Man beer, one I could have a few pints of, of an evening. If you’re coming round, I won’t say no to this accompanying you

title: Long Blonde
description: I didn’t care much for this hoppy, light beer from Long Man Brewery. It was too hoppy for me

title: Birra de Natalie
description: My 200th beer and what a beauty it was. Bought from Genoa for my parents (they were kind enough to share it with me) this sneaks up on you. The first step is sharp - the 8% cutting through, but the more sips you take, the smoother and tastier it gets; it’s fruity too. Lovely beer

title: Homebrew
description: Got a brewing kit last Christmas and made this brew for my birthday in August. It made 40 pints! Gave a lot a way but found another 7 pints under the stairs the other day. This beer was OK but has an odd aftertaste. It gets an extra point for the good reviews from everyone else, but I think I’m going to stick to drinking other people’s beer

title: KingGoblin
description: You can taste the 6.6% of this monster. Full of flavour but it’s an evening sipper

title: 5am Saint
description: At last! A @brewdogofficial beer with some good taste. Enjoyed this one and would consider picking it up for a “party beer”. Not one I would have at home again though.

title: Dead Pony Club
description: If you want a Brewdog beer that tastes better than the Punk IPA (#195) then this is it. Although, it’s not good enough to gain any more points. It’s still a hipster lager parading itself as an IPA. I expected better from this big name brand

title: Punk IPA
description: A “post modern classic”. Brewdog came out of nowhere a couple of years ago. Hipster beer with not much flavour. Inoffensive but doesn’t make you go “wow”. There is a hoppy taste there somewhere but watered down. Would have again if it was the only beer on offer

title: Heritage Suffolk Pale Ale
description: First beer of the year and a good one to boot. Second beer in the Heritage series and they are still onto a winner. Quite a lovely beer this one and went down well. Was a good colour and lovely flavour. Despite being light and hoppy it had a lovely taste

title: Heritage Vintage Fine Ale
description: A couple of these bottle conditioned beers from @greenekingbrewery appeared in Tesco the other day. A full pint (568ml) and tasty. Believe it would be called a complex flavour. Certainly a slow drinker kind of beer - especially at 6.5%! Would have again

title: Pale Ale
description: Not heard of “My Generation” brewery before. Although a pale ale, the flavour of the hops were strong. I’m fond of this beer, and would happily drink this all evening

title: Milk Stout
description: My final one from Christmas day (a very much #latergram, I know). Quite a strong heavy beer - not one for me. Had quite a strong coffee taste and, despite loving coffee, it wasn’t to my liking

title: Budding Pale Ale
description: Not as nice as the first one but still pleasant

title: Black IPA
description: I’m slowly becoming a fan of black IPA beers as they’re becoming more and more popular. Very tasty beers

title: Heritage
description: First Christmas beer and a good one to start with. Good body and nice flavour

title: Evil Elf
description: Was disappointed about this supposedly festive beer. Although noticed that it is “tropical” apparently. a bit better than the beer the other day. Not enough for an extra point

title: Sweet Stout
description: my final beer from Genoa. A nice evening beer, complex stout flavours without being heavy. Definitely a slow sipper

title: Hallucination Brune
description: I only found out about @loudshirtbeer today, so had to hunt down one of their beers. What a fantastic pint. Well flavoured without being overbearing. Looking forward to trying out some of their other ones!

title: Jeffrey Hudson Bitter JHB
description: What a Rod Stewart of a beer. Not horrible but nothing there to like. Plain, simple with a hint of taste. Middle of the road

title: Ambrata
description: Another Genoa Ale - very compatible in flavour with the previous one but 7%! Very enjoyable, shame it’s all the way in Italy!

title: Amber Ale
description: One of three beers all the way from Genoa. After putting up the Christmas tree, this little wonder (along with matching glass) was a perfect way to finish the day. It’s like a hipster craft ale but tasty

title: Summit
description: Light, crisp, tasty. Drank this without questioning its flavour.

title: Victory Ale
description: Lovely taste when you first take a sip but then an appalling after-taste bombards your mouth. Managed to finish it but I do not know how.

title: Christmas Ale
description: it’s December so I was able to crack open this beer - accompanied by some co-op Brie and cranberry tree crisps. Nice beer, not as Christmassy tasting as the one from Harvey’s but went down pleasantly. Lasted the whole evening.

title: Wolf Rock
description: @sharpsbrewery is another brewery that struggles to make a bad beer. Wonderful red IPA. Went down very easily.

title: Trooper
description: Drank this with not much thought. Just tasted like a bog standard bitter. Would drink again but wouldn’t hunt it down

title: Session IPA
description: Just No. The first split second hints that this beer might be palatable but after that, it descends into a whirlwind of disappointment and dishwasher water

title: Gem
description: A nice, tasty beer. Simple

title: Special London Ale
description: What a delightful beer. Light but complex. @youngsbeer have really knocked this one out the park

title: Boadicea
description: managed to open and pour this beer with one hand (long story) but a nice refreshing beer. Once again, surprised by the colour producing a tasty beer

title: Southwold Pale Ale
description: I was immediately put off by this beer due to the near transparent colour. However, I was wrong. A smooth refreshing pale ale that went down swimmingly

title: Ruddles Best
description: If this was meant to be their best, I would hate to try their worst. Weirdly watery and severely lacking any sort of aftertaste

title: Jester IPA
description: Lovely smooth tasty beer brewed by @adnams for Marks and Spencer. Went down rather quick, so it must have been good!

title: American Pale
description: quite a nice hipster beer. Could have a few of these although it’s missing some depth.

title: Sea Fury
description: lovely amber ale from @sharpsbrewery. Great way to end a lovely weekend

title: 80 Shilling
description: Smooth & tasty. Everything you want in a beer.

title: Electric Boogaloo
description: Another beer from @flavourlyhq and although not strictly an ale, I thought I would give it ago. It’s a mid-range beer - better than a lot of lagers but nowhere near as good as an ale. Has flavour but a bit bland for me.

title: Sunburst
description: Lovely balanced beer from @darkstarbrewco. Perfect end to the week. Could sit and drink this all evening.

title: Pillow fight
description: A chocolate marshmallow stout from @flavourlyhq. It’s...ok. There hints of chocolate (not enough), hints of marshmallow (when you really try) and many hints of a bit bland. A slow sipper

title: Flamingo Juice
description: I purposely signed up to a new beer service just to get some more beers and this was the first one out. I was instantly dubious based on the last tropical beer experience (see number 152 “Tart”). However I was pleasantly surprised and have drunk this rather quickly (might have something to do with my pedal falling off)

title: Circadian
description: Another smashing beer from @ringwoodbrewery. As far as I am concerned they can do no wrong

title: Chimera
description: Wasn’t expecting the best from this and its a bit better than expected. Crushed can as I forgot to take the photo. 🤦‍♂️

title: Arrowaine
description: This dark beer looked like a stout but tasted more like the black IPAs that have been cropping up. Chocolate undertones but a bit too hard nosed for my liking

title: Sticky Toffee Pudding Ale
description: (Brewery previously was Wells) Certainly a post-dinner beer this one. Heavy, sweet and slow drinking, but full of taste. A one-off

title: Darwin’s Origin
description: Forgot to take a photo straight away. Another birthday beer, but one I’m not too keen on. Drinkable but wouldn’t have another.

title: Elvis Juice
description: After the last citrus beer, I was dubious about this one. Grapefruit is definitely available to taste. First sip was meh, at a 4, but the more I drank the more I liked it, to the point where I would give it a

title: Dr. Thirsty’s
description: Next up from @wychwoodgoblins is this blonde beer. Was light, crisp and tasty. And went down a treat after riding 100 miles today

title: Goliath
description: what a nicely balanced crisp beer. Very drinkable

title: Tart
description: Wow. This. Is. Tart. I was not expecting the sharp citrus, cider-like taste. It. Is. Foul. Two sips and I’m out. We’ve actually managed to find a beer my wife would drink though. And that’s because it’s not a beer at all. although I’ve had to pour it away it gets an extra point for someone in this house declaring it drinkable.

title: Original
description: A classic bitter. Small glass for the groom to be tomorrow

title: Good Old Boy
description: When I started drinking, I used to have a pint of lager and my dad would show so much disappointment on his face. When I finally got into ales in my twenties, I would drink the light hoppy floral numbers. This beer was bought for my 30th birthday and it is hard to beat. Full bodied bitter with a lovely taste. I’ll be onto stouts next decade 😉 fantastic beer. More please!

title: Forty Niner
description: Not intentional that this @ringwoodbrewery beer ended up as 149! This beer really made me realise how much I love Ringwood beers. Consistently excellent taste, they are fast approaching as one of my favourite breweries. This is a cracking bitter and it is hard to criticise (and yes that is an @ale_house_rock stamp on the side of the bottle)

title: Session Pale Ale
description: A generic, hipster, tasteless, craft beer. No taste, no offensiveness.

title: Yakima Red
description: Keeping me company while playing a game of Pandemic. A classic craft ale taste. Drinkable

title: Seven Souls
description: Another birthday beer (there was a lot of birthday beer, and will be a lot of reviews heading this way). Dark, chocolatey but not to my taste. Slow sipper, and better than some but wouldn’t rush to have it again. A heavy drink

title: Fire Catcher
description: A tasty golden ale from a birthday box of beer, drunk out of a new jug just for the occasion

title: Devils Backbone
description: This standard IPA is has got a cool name (although missing an apostrophe 🤔) but an average tase. Could easily drink several of an evening, but not an exceptional beer

title: Pähklipurt
description: The last one from the craft ale box I ordered and what a disappointment to end on. You can taste the coffee and chocolate in this stout but it tastes so disgusting. It’s rare I have to throw a beer away but this is one of them. I tried it several times over the evening but every time it left a horrible taste in my mouth. Down the drain

title: Zig
description: A Belgian beer from the craft beer delivery. Not offensive, but I’ve certainly had Belgian beers with more taste. Seems to be a string of 6, but

title: Trafalgar
description: A present from life long Ale House Rock fans @geoffst and @mj.street. From the Chatham Historic Dockyard, this bottle conditioned ale is a nice drink. A bit of an odd after taste but the after-after taste Improves

title: Apricot Wheat
description: Yet another beer from the same place as the last couple. They seem to be getting better. Can’t taste the apricot, so just a sweet wheat beer.

title: Sorachi Saison
description: Finally, a craft beer with a bit of taste. Drunk after bottling 40 bottles of home brew, this was refreshing. When I said a bit of taste, I meant a bit. Might actually consider having this again if there was nothing better on offer

title: Simko Eil
description: I got a craft beer box and this is the second one out. Much like the first (#137), tasteless and meh
A generic, weak tasting lager-esque beer. Managed to finish but has left an odd aftertaste and an unimpressive finish

title: Gardeners Tipple
description: This was a lovely beer. Not sure it would be ideal for a post-garden work drink, but tasty regardless

title: Folded Mountains
description: Generic pale ale from @aldiuk. Would be good as a BBQ companion, although not as flavourful as some others

title: Montana Red
description: A red ale with a cheap aftertaste. From Waitrose, this by no means carries the premium label that is normally associated with the shop. I wouldn’t buy this again

title: EPA
description: This 6.5% snuck into a batch from Saltdean Brewery. Tasted beautiful and perfect after gardening in the rain. Slightly floral taste (maybe?) but was good non-the-less

title: Wainwright The Golden Ale
description: Clean, crisp, refreshing and a great post-stag beer. Would happily have a few of these

title: Beyond the Pale
description: what a disappointing beer. Shared with a friend, this hipster craft beer was nothing but meh. Glad I only had half a can

title: Pilsner
description: this beer is from the same brewery as Hop House and Guinness. I did pie some into a shot glass to show the colour but I then forgot to take a picture. Slightly watery in comparison to hop house and not as much flavour. Won’t be buying again

title: Cocker Hoop
description: A crisp, clean, refreshing beer

title: Golden Goal
description: just before kick-off I poured this little number and must have bought good luck! It’s half time and at least England are playing better than this tastes. It’s ok but a bit watery

title: Pale
description: Another one from The Rev James. Crisp and light but a bit watery

title: Golden Ale
description: Confession, this isn’t the bottle nor the day I drank this, however I needed something to represent this beer as I forgot to take a photo but I’ve been thinking about it. A lot. Another one from the Saltdean Brewery (of “Drunk Monk” #101 and ESB #108 fame), I drank their Golden Ale at the weekend. Now, I don’t know if this was situational bias (it was sunny and I’d just cooked an amazing BBQ for some wonderful friends) but this beer hit the spot. It was beautiful, well balanced and horrifically tasty. I could have sat and drunk it all afternoon. At 4.5% too, I could have done. Easily one of the best beers I’ve ever had. On top of all that, I was told today that it was the last one - so it gets a bonus exclusivity point.

title: The Governor
description: A bit watery and tasteless

title: Ripsaw
description: forgot to post this tasty number from the makers of Hobgoblin the other day. Lovely sipper while listening to football and watching @fiaworldrx.

title: XB
description: Light, crisp and great for drinking while watching sport. What have I become?!

title: Golden Host
description: Although not the best beer, very drinkable.

title: Unicorn
description: Had this the other day as a celebration. Can’t remember what it tasted like but can remember it was pretty good

title: Premium Best Bitter
description: Lovely standard beer. Does the job

title: Humdinger
description: Well this is a humdinger of beer. Lovely and crisp with a honey aftertaste. Tasty.

title: Session Pale
description: Far too hoppy for me. Struggled through this one

title: Velo
description: A nice crisp beer from @blacksheepbrewery . Perfect after a long drive and a lovely greeting for holiday

title: Hop House Lager
description: From the makers of @guinness, this lager is very similar to the pilsner that came before. Not much flavour but great on a hot day next to the BBQ, or in the garden or while you’re doing DIY.

title: The 1079 Project
description: I bought a 4 pack from Aldi of this pilsner. Perfect with a BBQ or on a hot summers day - neither of which I have - so post DIY will have to do. Crisp and refreshing although not an ale

title: India Pale Ale
description: This lush beer from @shepherdneame was a great second pint. Good body but not too heavy.

title: Goram
description: This light ale is lovely (despite the rude Brewery name @butcombe) and crisp. Refreshing and tasty

title: Prince of Denmark
description: This 7.5% is a dark syrupy Christmas style ale. Heavy and certainly not a summer ale or one for a warm day. Coffee under/over tones. Fortunately sharing with @geoffst so only need drink half of this half pint.

title: Gunpowder IPA
description: Light, wonderfully balanced ale that easily went down and doesn’t have the whiskey undertones. A cracking drink from Innis and Gunn. Happily have plenty of these

title: Patronus
description: This is not a Harry Potter beer but instead a Weissbier. Probably my favourite wheat beer so far - it’s slightly fruity and lovely after a hard days decorating. Sun illuminated but golden nonetheless.

title: Spitfire
description: A staple, british bitter

title: ESB
description: The next beer from the Saltdean Brewery is an ESB. Lovely complex flavours which make a smooth and tasty bitter. Rather strong for an ale (not sure on the ABR but I can tell it’s not a walk in the park

title: Liberation I.P.A.
description: A big standard beer. Nothing to rave about, nothing to moan about

title: Herm Island Gold
description: This is a smooth light beer. Would happily have a full pint next time!

title: Wonky Donkey
description: This oddly-named beer has an odd logo and an odd taste. Brewed here in Guernsey, it’s drinkable, but I’m glad I’m only having half a pint. 4.5/10 from me, and a 6 from @geoffst.

title: Liberation Ale
description: Sharing this one with @geoffst on our Guernsey adventure. Light crisp beer that tangs the tongue. Makes your mouth a bit puckered. It gets a 7/10 from both of us.

title: Lancaster Bomber
description: Smells like an old man pub and tastes like it belongs there. Not in a bad way! Just an average beer

title: The Original
description: Picked this up in Lidl, haven’t had one for years. These are beautifully, oaky, scotchy and easily drinkable. Brewed in barrels of spirits and dangerously high in alcohol. Went down far too quickly

title: Drunk Monk
description: This “home brew” from the Saltdean Brewery was exchanged for some bike brake pads, which seems like a good deal. This is their stout and it is glorious. It’s got all the right flavours and smells without being too heavy. It’s chocolatey and coffee-y and tasty. Definitely a slow burner but would happily have this again and again

title: Medusa
description: What a fine beer for the century. An Aldi special, this ruby beer is perfect for a windy, rainy evening while watching Game of Thrones. My wife informs me Medusa had two sisters and was beautiful until she was cursed with ugliness for being a bitch. Who knew? 🤷‍♀️

title: Banana Bread Beer
description: (Brewery previously was Wells) Bought for me by @robynphillippa. An odd little beer. It tastes like you are taking a sip of beer and then eating one of those foam bananas after every mouthful. Subtle banana taste but mainly beer. Good, and would have it again, but wouldn’t rush to buy it

title: Blonde
description: I’m back! After moving house and not having internet, I took a break from posting. But I’m back. Blonde beer that is easy to drink. Wouldn’t buy it but it got left at my house. Quaffable

title: Pullman
description: A light hoppy fizzy beer. Going down very well, great with a heavy dinner!

title: Rye
description: This full bodied ale was a perfect slow sipping beer. Lovely flavours without being too heavy

title: Mr Trotter’s Great British Chestnut Ale
description: This is an odd one, plenty of flavour with a watery aftertaste. However, the more I drank it, the nicer it got

title: Bitter and Twisted
description: A light crisp beer, nice citrus notes. Would have again

title: Original
description: Apparently I would be rewarded with the finish of this beer. I was mildly satisfied. Drinkable but nothing special.

title: T.E.A.
description: A good old fashioned winter evening ale. Great for sitting indoors in front of the TV. Lovely.

title: Whiskey Edition
description: An odd combination of beer and whiskey- what a way to see in the new year. Strong, dark and whiskey undertones

title: Tunnel Vision
description: A simple, slightly watery ale, but alright.

title: Bitter
description: Always wanted to try one of these @asda beers. 4 for 90p. Didn’t have high hopes and it was exactly as I expected. Soapy, watery nothingness. Had to pour it away.

title: Christmas Ale
description: This 7.5% power house is definitely a sipper. So far taken an hour to drink half of it. It’s a heavy Christmas day in a glass, but by golly it’s wonderful. A half pint is more than enough and you certainly wouldn’t want it on a night out!

title: Guinness Golden Ale
description: I’m not really a fan of original Guinness but this not-so-golden ale is tasty

title: Broadside
description: This 6.3% beer commemorates the battle of Solebay in 1672 apparently. If the battle was anything like the beer, it was a dark, strong, chocolatey fight which was slow and heavy. Certainly not one to be repeated! I feel full up after a few sips already!

title: Waggle Dance
description: (Brewery previously was Wells) A light refreshing, slightly flavourless Ale. Alright with some food I suppose?

title: Southwold
description: In the pub, this Adnams beer had an odd aftertaste. Turns out it’s “distinctively hoppy”

title: London Pale Ale
description: a lighter, hoppy beer. Not the nicest but will suffice as a Light post-dinner sipple

title: Deuchars
description: a fruity, full-bodied malty beer. Tastes darker than it appears

title: Dunkel Fester
description: A dark coffee beer perfect for board games and post-dinner slump

title: Cornish Knocker
description: Comical story on the back with an average beer to boot

title: Betty Stogs
description: A malty beer which has slowed my drinking pace but still tasty

title: Hops ‘n’ Honey
description: A light, easy drinkin’ ale

title: Champion
description: a dark caramel-y kind of beer. 7.3%! Lovely and light despite its colour

title: Citra IPA
description: Nice, light, inoffensive beer. Went down easily with no scrunching of this face. Yes I know it’s Wednesday but I had to review some words I had written and needed to get through it. Would have again.

title: Cambridgeshire Golden Ale
description: This beer got better as I drunk it. Starting at a 4. It’s light and crisp and finished on a

title: White IPA
description: A much better beer. Perfect to wash down a Dominos pizza. Light, a little lacking in flavour but that’s to be expected for an IPA

title: Belgian Wheat Beer
description: Been intrigued to try this one since @mj.street and @geoffst bought it for me (along with the other m&s beers which have adorned my feed of late). Apparently brewed with orange peel and coriander, it seems to taste just like any other wheat beer. Maybe it has some slight citrus undertones...? Certainly makes me feel like I’m on the continent. It’s drinkable but not sure I’d rush to have it again.

title: Cascade
description: Another M&S beer, this time a bit better. Sweet and fizzy, this 5% isn’t too bad. But too light for my liking but good nonetheless. Was going to give it 6 but it’s a bit too gassy.

title: Southwold Winter IPA
description: Brewed by Adnams for Marks & Spencer. Tasting it I can see why they don’t sell it under the “Adnams” name. It’s certainly not up to scratch and doesn’t compare to their other beers (was drinking a Ghost ship just yesterday). Very floral with an odd aftertaste. Wouldn’t buy it again.

title: Triple Hop
description: I find myself opting to buy beers I’ve never had before over beers I love just for this account. Another co-op one which was very quaffable and went down very quickly.(although pricey for what it was)

title: Golden Ale
description: an easy drinking beer from co-op. Apparently perfect for a summer garden, but an autumnal kitchen is just fine too. A little too fizzy for me

title: 44 Northcote
description: GBK’s very own pale ale.  It’s sweet, refreshing, light. Good choice to go with a burger, even if it was on the pricey side.

title: Innis & None
description: There has been a plethora of non-alcoholic beers appearing recently, so thought I would give one a go from a brewery I've always admired. A bit of an odd after taste, but just drinkable. Took me nearly 2 hours to finish... won't be buying this one again

title: EPA
description: Excuse the glass (and the courgette). A light beer, missing a lot of flavour, really. Ok, I suppose
The last beer of my Dorset trip. This corker from @palmersbrewery is very tasty. At 5% it's bordering on dangerous but is going down well. Well worth a sip if you get chance!

title: Dorset Gold
description: Forgot to post this from Thursday. Very tasty, easy drinking beer. Could easily have sipped it all night, pint after pint but unfortunately I only had one, which went down very easy.

title: 61 Deep
description: This beer has been cropping up a lot recently so I thought I would give it a go. It's disappointing. A light, flavourless beer that's taken a while to drink. I would avoid it, really

title: Fursty Ferret
description: Pint perfection. The 10 I've been chasing. This is what every bottled beer should strive to be. What better way to end my holiday in the West Country then with a beer hailing from the very place

title: Firkin Fox
description: Another corker of a beer from @badgerbeers. They can do no wrong (except with the ginger one 😉). Very enjoyable and will be in my trolley if I see it again! This was obtained from the Hall and Woodhouse brewery shop

title: Two Hoots
description: A light beer for the beautiful weather, going down far too quickly. After the Hall and Woodhouse brewery tour today it's only fitting to have a beer. Although the badger ones were not cold enough to drink

title: Butty Bach
description: Despite a rude sounding name (which is an attribute I often avoid in beers), this pint went down a lot quicker and easier than anticipated. A good pint (P.s. Take note of @laurzstreet in the background drinking wine off the table... #classiclaurz)

title: Cumberland
description: just a bog standard beer. Good staple pint

title: Rye
description: An apparently pale ale which is a tropical and citrus beer. It's getting better with every sip. An odd one, but well "needed"

title: Old Ale
description: Such an odd choice of beer after sitting in the sun for a couple of hours. Didn't realise it was a "winter" Ale (I thought the waitress could have warned me!). Tasty nonetheless and went down rather well actually!

title: Golden Bolt
description: A light golden crisp Ale. Nothing to shout about, not disappointing though!

title: Bravaria
description: this non-alcoholic wheat beer (yes, 0%) was gifted to me by my father to try. It's odd. I've been sipping it all night and can't quite figure it out. It's nice but not amazing

title: Gold
description: This was so tasty I forgot to take a picture! Crisp, light, wonderful

title: Golden Champion
description: A crisp golden ale from my favourite brewers @badgerbeer. Beautiful and refreshing after a day walking round Brighton

title: Ghost Ship
description: a light tasty refreshing beer. Perfect for sitting on a campsite with the wife and in-laws

title: Old Crafty Hen
description: My 49th beer and a good one too. The big brother of the Old Speckled Hen. Dangerously strong and full of flavour

title: Audit Ale
description: Out of the 3 craft beers I bought, this was my favourite. Drank it out the bottle hence the shot of beer to show the colour. Went down far to quickly and easily for a beer that was 6.8%!

title: Cheeky
description: another craft beer, this one bottle conditioned. Slight better than the other day but still wouldn't buy another

title: Ace Edge
description: A cheeky tipple on a Tuesday with this craft beer. It tastes like I should have been sitting in brewdog which is not something I particularly enjoy. Still, it was ok

title: Oxford Gold
description: Sorry about the Carling glass. I thoroughly enjoy Brakspear beers and this is no different. Light and full of goodness

title: Bombardier
description: (Brewery previously was Wells) Shamefully out the can for this one and for the I apologise. A dark, tasty malty beer, but average in the beer world. Good for tiding over until payday

title: Organic Ale
description: What better way to end this holy weekend with a beer from God himself. Had such a busy day hunting for eggs in my parents garden before being presented with this beer instead of chocolate (they look after my teeth and health my mum and dad do!). It's crisp, light and golden (not normally a colour I would go for) but good for sipping of an evening.

title: Hobgoblin
description: Back home and this beer welcomed me with warm open arms. It takes a particular time and feeing to have a Hobgoblin but this is it. TV, crackers and a dark warming beer. Complex, heavy taste but one that's great

title: Willemoes
description: I've had a few beers while I've been in Denmark 🇩🇰 but this is the only one I've bought "home" for an evening in (on that note "Ale 16" is very popular over here and great 👍). Back to this beer, it's got a bit of a head as I was dancing with it, but it is very tasty and would recommend it if you find yourself in this fine country

title: Old Speckled Hen
description: A good entry-level bitter which fulfils every need for beer. A good all-rounder and Cask Marque's beer of the week!

title: Doombar
description: One of my favourite beers on tap (although recently it seems to have lost its way). A standard bitter, a good malt

title: Hop Garden Gold
description: A light refreshing golden beer. Easy to drink, easy to please. Would not be disappointed if you bought me a pint of this

title: Weissbier
description: A German wheat beer that seems to be everywhere these days. It's ok. Weirdly perfumed and wouldn't have one again

title: Bombardier Burning Gold
description: (Brewery previously was Wells) a great crisp beer from Bombardier. Better than their original one in my opinion!

title: Foundation stone
description: Probably my favourite of the Lymestone Brewery trio. Slightly citrusy and an all round nice beer!

title: Bah Humbug
description: Yes, I know this is a Christmas beer. It due to the "no home drinking" January it's only just been opened. It tastes like Christmas (which I suppose is a good thing). It's a slow supper but tasty

title: Hobgoblin Gold
description: An unusually light one from @hobgoblin_beer but still so very tasty. Had to take this quick as you can see it's already going down well. Not hoppy but light to drink. Give me another!

title: Weissbier
description: I've been meaning to post this for a week! Another German one, a lager of f sorts with plenty of taste. Went down very easily and quickly while I was fixing my coffee machine. Bottle conditioned (hence not the full pint) but would happily have it again - preferably on a summers day in the garden

title: Stone Faced
description: An "in-your-face" beer that taste stronger than its 4% label. A bit of an "off" aftertaste. I'll finish it though

title: Wild Wader
description: I took a break from home drinking for (most of) January so sorry for the radio silence, although I ended up in the pub more often. This one has been in my cupboard for a month and I've been waiting to crack it open. It's a tasty, tasty beer - hopping on the heels of the Fursty Ferret (the best bottled beer) from the same brewer. I would happily buy this again. Throughly recommended! Slightly citrusy with a mix of hops

title: Triple Hopped IPA
description: I started drinking this before the picture was taken. It must be good

title: WeihnachtsBier
description: More flavour than the colour lets on. Tasty and quaffable

title: Abdominal Stoneman
description: The #aleadvent is over, but I'm going to keep track of some of the beers I drink from time to time. This 7% (!) beer is a strong full bodied Ale. Small brewery from Staffordshire - a Christmas present! I'm off to watch Trainspotting with it and relate to the "hit" they keep mentioning

title: Sussex Golden Ale
description: Light, tasty and perfect for a lunchtime, pre roast sip. Great last beer

title: Whistable Bay
description: A sweet, golden ale with flavour and character. Drinkable. Very drinkable.

title: Cornish Red Ale
description: Bottle conditioned red beer. Tasty, bit watery but good flavours

title: The Amber Adder
description: Oh my god. That is terrible. I've had to pour it away as I can't finish it.

title: Amarillo Golden Ale
description: Hoppy. Alright I suppose. Weird aftertaste.

title: Sussex IPA
description: A hoppy number. Not really my cup of tea. Didn't finish it, but probably could have.

title: Courage
description: Firstly I have a couple of apologies to get out the way! 1) sorry for being a day late (was busy coming second in a pub quiz yesterday) 2) sorry for the poor lighting in the photo - I'm in the garage 😀 Anyway, on with the beer. It's a rock solid taste. Can't go wrong. Would drink for days!
A dark malty beer, great for sipping while wrapping presents and putting up a Christmas tree. Wouldn't want it in a pub - too heavy for a night out!

title: Golden Ale
description: A light, fizzy, hoppy ale. Not really a fan, but drinkable.

title: Pure UBU.
description: A brother of the Mad Goose on day 1. Tastier, more malty & more full bodied. A good beer, would happily drink several.

title: Master Brew
description: A classic beer if ever there was one. A bitter Ale.

title: Old Thumper
description: Another classic from Ringwood. Darker and fuller than the Boon, but not quite as tasty.

title: Greenwich Red Ale
description: A good beer. Would have again. Malty and perfect for a night in with rain hammering the windows.

title: Green Gecko
description: A pleasant beer. Has an odd twang but could drink all evening.

title: Amber Bitter
description: A good beer that is 89p from Aldi. Enough said.

title: Greenwich Black IPA
description: Unfortunately in a plastic glass 😟 but a tasty beer. It does indeed taste like IPA and is indeed black. Ok as a beer I suppose!

title: The Golden Goose
description: A simple inoffensive beer. Nothing more really to say!

title: Yorkshire Gold
description: A light, crisp beer that seems to be going down rather well. Not offensive but not the #bestbitter

title: 9 Hop
description: Hmm, 9 Hop is not as good as the 31 Hop from the other day. Although more hoppy (so not as tasty). I can manage it. Wouldn't have I again.

title: Ruby Rooster
description: More drinkable than the colour implies. A malty tasty beer.

title: 31 Hop
description: A light citrus flavoured beer. Despite 31 hops it's not horrifically hoppy (not a fan of to many hops). A tasty beer indeed. It says to have it with curry, which is what I'm doing

title: Boon Doggle
description: Tasty. A tasty tasty drinkable beer. Give me more

title: Wild Bills IPA
description: Not part of the #aleadvent but a very good beer. Good session Ale I could sit and drink for hours. A tickle of hops.

title: The Purple Panther Porter
description: A dark, heavy beer. Wouldn't want a second pint of this. Might struggle through the first. Tastes of coffee and other things. Hmm

title: Hen's Tooth
description: From the same people as old speckled hen, this bottle conditioned malty Ale is a perfect beer for a night in. Strong flavours, ideal for sipping.

title: Mad Goose
description: A light, hoppy beer. Could easily sit in a pub and drink several pints - not really the kind of beer you'd sip while watching tv at home though.
